title: AITA for withholding information about his sexual health?
text:
I’ve been with three people this year. Currently in a relationship with someone who lives in Europe but we’ve had sex once unprotected (Kevin). Dated someone for 6+ months and had unprotected sex a few times during that time period (James). Dated someone else for a couple months but only had protected sex (Elijah).

As it turns out i got an STI, not anything serious and I’ve already been treated. It’s a first and I’m really put out by it, initially it’s something Kevin and I agreed to do both before I visit him later this month. After realizing, I sort of am suspicious he gave it to me but i don’t want to blame him and be wrong so I’ve let him know and told him to get checked. I also called the other person I’ve slept with unprotected and asked when they last got tested and told them to do it again to be sure. 

Partially, i focused on these two because they’re the most likely sources but also I’m on decent enough terms to call. However, James is... it’s less likely he’s the person i contracted it from but i have no way of knowing. Granted James and Elijah were both seeing other people, but i don’t want to talk to Elijah at all. He’s been harassing me constantly since we broke up to the point where my dad was set on calling the police on my behalf if he didn’t stop. I blocked him and he’d use other numbers or private his number to get in contact and it was very stressful. He only stopped very recently and to this day i worry that a random number is him going back on his word and continuing his harassment. He has the lowest risk and obviously if both James and Kevin don’t have it, he does and I need to contact him but AITA for waiting it out and telling him when i really and truly have to? 

((This may come up, but I’ve been tested while with James (first partner) and I was fine, possible he contracted it afterwards and passed it to me. Or that the first time I did it with Kevin I got it from him. I just have no way of knowing presently where it started))

title: AITA or am I justified in my decision?
text:
I have a to fraught history with my SIL due in part to my non-communication with her mother. 
I have not openly spoken to SIL since a single question at my wedding 6 years ago. Then out of nowhere she wanted to meet up for dinner. 
I am a big believer in awkwardness and adventure, so I said yes. Not out of any desire to rekindle an acquaintance but curiosity to see "the catch". 
A week or two later I am invited to her baby shower. 

Then Xmas comes and my husband goes to have lunch with SIL & MIL. SIL asks if I am going to her baby shower to which my husband says I can't make it. 
Then SIL goes off about how it's "her" day and I shouldn't put my issues with MIL in the way. And why can't I go if her divorced parents can be in the same room but I can't. Etc. 

My decision to not attend was due to the lack of emotional connection to SIL, the fact I am child free by choice, and because my MIL will turn it into a narcissist circus which I feel is not fair to SIL. 
Am I the araehole for not attending?

Not sorry- on mobile, crap writing structure, but edited for readability.

title: WIBTA for blatantly favoring my neice over my nephew?
text:
There's a lot of backstory, but essentially my(40F) kids (3rd-8th grade) are friends with their cousins who live close by. My neice is 12 and does normal 12-year-old kid stuff, which is to say that occasionally we need to address a behavior, but it's usually within the range of normal for her age. 

Her brother is almost 10 and is terrible to have over. He has physically hurt my youngest child on a number of occasions, has intentionally broken my kids' things because he was mad, does not follow our house rules, does not listen to me, etc.

We haven't had them over in a very long time because of differing comfort levels with pandemic safety. Now that everyone in our house is eligible for a vaccine, we will be going back to more gatherings, including with this family. 

Nephew has already repeatedly asked to be allowed to come over, spend the night, etc. I've had a year and a half away from his pretty awful behavior and I have zero desire to invite all of that back into my house. 

Neice also wants to come over, and I'm fine with that. I generally don't have to intervene, worry that she will hurt someone, worry that she will break things, etc.

Of course this is a problem. Even before we were separated from them for a long time, we had problems with making him sit out some play gatherings as a consequence of his behavior. Mom and dad don't let big sister come over if little brother can't come because they "don't want to listen to him whine about it", which is on par with how the behaviors have been handled at home. 

So we punish everyone or no one, which seems wildly unfair and unhelpful to be. I really think he should be let to feel the consequences of his behavior by knowing she is invited but not him because she can treat other people well.

WIBTA if I push this hard and exclude him as much as possible but not my neice? Obviously excluding a child sounds pretty terrible. But also I have my own kids to protect and frankly I ran out of patience with him years ago. I shouldn't have to be stressed in my own home about whether my kids will be hurt by their cousin. 

As far as I know, there is no neurodivergence. It seems like he's mostly just spoiled. I know that's not his fault, but I also do not want to deal with it anymore in my house.

title: AITA for not doing the dishes?
text:
I (26f) have been dating my bf (36m) for over 4 years now and i love him and his family very much. They have always been very kind. 

As someone with social anxiety, and claustrophobia when it comes to being surrounded by people (not sure if that is even a thing) big events are always a bit of a stressor, but I get through it. I always try to go out of my way to be more outgoing than I am comfortable with since I am still a little worried of coming off as aviodant to his family. Honestly it is kinda draining to keep that up for hours on end anyway.

When it comes to after dinner clean up, I ALWAYS help clear the table and bring stuff into the kitchen, which I know isnt much. Also let me tell you I have tried to do the dishes there, which they gladly accept the help. I guess for me it is an combo issue of: 1.) being in an area surrounded by people 2.) Not knowing where everything goes since this is not my house 3.) Even though she is helping and his mother is very sweet, she is the watch you while you work type of person which drives my anxiety and 4.) will kindly but constantly correct you if you so much as put something in the dishwasher wrong. All in all if i decide to do the dishes i pretty much have to take a nap afterward to calm down which is the time that the family will play games and have a few drinks which is more relaxed and obviously something i dont want to miss since that is the time i can feel more like myself with them.

On occasion while the  women are cleaning, some will make comments that people should be helping clean that werent cooking. I would love to just wait a few minutes for it to calm down so i can do it by myself but his mother will not leave the kitchen until they are done, and since she is in there, her daughters are in there which just is too many ppl in my opinion for a fairly small space. And for the record, i love to cook and would much rather do that but as the matriarch his mother likes to take the reigns with that as well. 

For those people that dont suffer from anxiety, I know how much of an excuse this sounds like, and i think that is what concerns me, having to tell his whole family my personal struggles, especially with something so minor. After trying my best to be outgoing all day when i am a true introvert i have on several occasions just sat with my bf instead of helping, embarrassed. I know i am kinda the asshole since they dont deserve to do all the work. Should i just do it anyway...? 
What do I do? AITA for rarely doing dishes?

title: AITA: Asking my bf to stop hanging out with someone?
text: So backstory, almost two years ago I met my ex at school on the west coast though a mutual friend (this friend lives on the west coast). We hit it off and started talking right before covid hit. Our mutual friend (21f) was very jealous of our relationship and avidly tried to make moves on him and break us up almost all through our relationship. We both moved back across the country (my ex is from ny and I’m from md) and we did long distance up until about 2 months ago, when we both moved back across the country for school. We are on a break right now but plan to get back together. Recently our mutual friend and my ex have been hanging out a lot and she’s been posting him all over her socials. It’s making me pretty uncomfortable especially knowing she’s been waiting for us to break up for a while. Am I the asshole if, when we get back together, I ask him to stop being friends with her or at least stop hanging out with her? I feel like this is a boundary for me but I don’t know if it’s a valid one?

title: AITA For not wanting to spend time with dad?
text:
I'll be brief because I've been up for an hour and 30 minutes just on pure rage alone

My brother wants to do something special for fathers day but an opportunity arose that was kinda perfect for this occasion, an orchestra is in town that my dad happens to love. My brother wants me to pay for my own ticket to go see this concert.

Normally that's understandable, but I haven't had a job because I've been focusing on college. And he's chastising me for not having money when all of the money I have goes straight into books food and travel. Am I the asshole for not wanting to spend money to go to a concert just to make dad happy?

title: AITA for saying my fianceé deserved the ticket?
text:
So my (24f) fianceé (26f) went on a drive with her car friends last weekend. I decided not to go because they all drive like maniacs when they get together. I'm a pretty anxious person so fast driving freaks me out.

So they are driving around pretty fast, the fastest guy is up front going around 80mph, my fianceé is last in line. There was 4 cars in the group.

So they're going around 80mph in a 50 zone. There's a cop. He puts his lights on, everyone slows down but my fianceé is the only one to pull over. She gets a fine for going 30 over. The cop said he clocked everyone's plates/speeds. So I imagine they're all gonna get tickets in the mail. But my fiancee got hers first since she actually stopped.

Fianceé comes home and is going off about how, "The cop clocked (guy who was in front) and not me! I was last so I wasn't going as fast." And I'm kinda shaking my head a little. I'm like, "Babe, I know how you all drive. The reason I stay home is because you all drive like lunatics and it freaks me out. Quite frankly, I think you guys deserve a reality check every once in a while."

She. Went. Off. Telling me how I wasn't there, so I wouldn't know how the situation played out (despite her being the one to relay the story to me), that I'm so hateful toward her hobby (cars), etc. This was the night it happened.

She stormed out of the house today because she brought up the fact that I said she deserved it and I said my opinion had not changed.

So, am I an asshole?

[UPDATE]: In the comments, I mentioned his she wants to fight the $180 USD ticket. She was encouraged by her car friends that she didn't deserve the ticket. I asked her how she plans to defend herself, and she said she's "just going to try her best" - she is not going to have a witness there, she has not called to have the officer's speed radar examined for faults, nothing. She's just gonna go before the judge with nothing to back her claims. 🙃 I am beyond frustrated.

title: AITA for almost never having time for a particular friend?
text: This has been eating me up ever since I started being friends with her. I love E so much, shes such an amazing friend, and honestly one of the few who don’t judge me based on my appearance. She still treats me like any of her other friends. Problem is, our lives are drastically different. While it’s normal for young adults to stay in their parents home (I’m in an asian community, both of us stay in our parents’ home), mine are extremely strict. I’m a migrant too, and due to laws I can’t work to move out yet either, hence if I have any interest in working and moving out I have to keep abiding my parents’ rules. Now E, Id say leads a pretty normal life. She even goes clubbing with her whole family and friends (excuse me if this is also normal lol as I said, strict parents = lame lifestyle). To hangout w/ others, I’d have to prep my parents for like 2-3 days to ASK, whereas all she has to do is give a text and she’s done. Recently, she has been saying how I’m ignoring her but she still sees me going out. I tend to gravitate towards other ppl who have strict parents generally bc they tend to understand what it’s like, so when I’m meeting them, they’re planned 7-10 days ahead, and bc so much effort goes into planning, we try to milk as much as we can from said day. Whereas E would ask to meetup anywhere between 2 hrs to 1-2 days ahead. I get that that’s what is normal, just make a plan and go but it’s just not the same with my household. And not to mention my spending money comes from my parents too so I can’t just say ok as I don’t want to borrow money. I’ve tried about 3 times, in the 3 yrs I’ve known her, to explain to her that our lifestyles aren’t just the same. She always says how she’s “done with people treating her this way”, but the thing is I’m not doing it purposely, I always respond in time it’s just that I’m almost never available for impromptu meetups. Who am I kidding, I can’t even just chill in my room for more than 2 hrs at a time, I hope you guys get the picture on how little freedom I get. But still, I can understand why she’s never get it because our families just aren’t the same. Recently I was sick of being caught up between my family and E, hence I cancelled a class 2 days ahead ahead, so that I could makeup for not going w/ her at all. Her texting became very dry, and almost like she doesn’t care anymore. I did wanna say “well if you’re not up for it we can meet some other time” but I don’t want her to think I’m making up excuses to not meet her (as she had previously). She said she wasn’t free, I insisted we meet, like a bestfriend would to another, and she responded like she just knew me for a month. I feel so shit. AITA?

title: AITA For Saying Sister Should Pay Her Way on Family Vacation?
text:
I (30F) have two younger sisters (A is 27, K is 24). My parents have a Disney timeshare and have started planning a week long family vacation now that there’s a light at the end of the pandemic tunnel. All three of us sisters are employed full-time, have significant others who are employed full-time, and all of us were lucky enough to not have our jobs or income affected by the pandemic. That will become relevant in just a minute. 

My mom, K, and I were talking about the trip and K mentioned how A’s significant other can’t come for work reasons and A might not come because she “doesn’t want to pay for a plane ticket and stuff”. My mom asked for clarification and started implying that she might just pay for her. This immediately annoyed me (for a few reasons) and I told my mom that it would be really unfair for her to pay A’s vacation expenses when K and I (plus our S/Os) are being expected to pay our way. In fact mom was damn near belligerent when she initially questioned K and I about whether we could afford to go. For whatever is matters, the plane and park tickets combined are looking to be ~$900/person. 

The last time my mom planned a trip to Disney, my husband (then fiancé) and I’s finances weren’t nearly as comfortable as they are now and mom made no offer to pay anything toward us going on the family vacation. In fact we were explicitly prohibited from coming because she’d decided that we needed to focus our money on buying a house nevermind that she knew we weren’t ready to consider one for multiple other reasons.

A and her S/O are even more well off financially than we are and they have almost no debt. There’s no reason that A can’t pay her way. K said that A wants to go on vacation, but just doesn’t want to pay for it. Well don’t we freaking all but that’s not how it works. Now mom is pissed at me and threatening to cancel the “family” part of the vacation and just go with friends. K thinks mom’s overreacting but also doesn’t think I shouldn’t have said anything. 

Was I wrong to bring it up? Am I the a-hole?

Edit to clarify some things: I’m not trying to tell my mom how to spend her money. At no point did I tell her “you can’t do this” nor will I. If she still chose to, I’d be upset but I’d move on. When I said earlier that I was annoyed for a few reasons, they’re mostly related to the blatant favoritism that A gets as a result of her spoiler, tantrum prone behavior. We’ve pointed out Mom’s biased treatment to her before which is at least part of the reason she got pissed.

title: AITA for not sharing?
text:
So... I have a condition where I have to drink about 200 ounces of water a day, a little more if I do physical activity. If I don’t I get horrible dehydration, migraines, nausea, pass out, and occasionally throw up. Well, I’m also in the BSA and we went on a hike one day. I brought a 2.5 liter Camelbak, and a 48 oz. Nalgene, both full, to the hike, and had a plan to drink some more water afterwards. The Hike was gonna be about 18 miles in total, and we would stop at the 10 mile marker where there was a park to eat lunch. When we got to Lunch, I ate, and loaded back up. Clipping my pack on and tying my shoes. Then, a young boy (11)came up to me. I notice he has no pack, Water bottle, or anything really.

YB: Can I have your Water

Me: Yeah... you can have a couple drinks

YB: No dummy... I want the WHOLE water bottle

Me: What?

YB: I want to drink ALL that Water

Me: Um... I’m sorry but I don’t think so

I go on to explain my condition and am PRAYING that I don’t get hellfire for this from the Scoutmaster, or this Young Boys mother. He ends up crying and trying to guilt trip me about how he is a low-income kid, and this is his first hike and he forgot his Water bottle. I am not easily persuaded, especially not by Cry baby’s. I was raised to be tough, and to help others as much as possible, without jeopardizing yourself. I was already getting dizzy and knew that if I gave him ALL my remaining water I would pass out and vomit down the trail. I ended up giving him about a 12 oz. drink, and I got a bad headache at the 14 mile marker. AITA for not giving him my Water?

Edit: The Scoutmaster made sure everyone had a bottle when we left that Friday, but YB left his at the Campsite when we started the hike. He had brought a Milk Jug full of Water.

title: AITA for being annoyed that this girl has given my girlfriend and I a racially charged nickname?
text:
My girlfriend and I are interracial she is mixed race and black and I am white. One of her female friends (who is also black) calls us a nickname of “Loving” (after “Loving vs Virginia”). I think this is partially because there are few interracial relationships at our school.

I think the girl means it as a term of endearment (and in a way it is quite sweet) but it still annoys me. Our relationship might be interracial and there are legacies but we are more than that. My girlfriend is a whole person and so am I and we love each other’s full characters very much. Do you think the girl will eventually get bored of using the nickname?

title: AITA for telling my 11-year-old nephew - who never committed a crime nor was ever arrested nor prosecuted - that he **does** have a criminal record, albeit a clean and blank one?
text:
This morning, my family and my girlfriend were talking to my 11-year-old nephew and out of intellectual curiosity of a topic, criminal records were brought up. My brother told his son that a criminal record is something that someone gets after being convicted of a crime, and it can make getting a job difficult. I then said that actually, everyone, including us has a criminal record, although we all have clean and blank criminal records. I explained that we have criminal records, but we do not have any *crimes on* our criminal records, and thus not less likely to get jobs. They just looked at me for a second and then said that that is not how it works and is not proper semantics and syntax.

I explained, “Well, we must have a certificate that affirmatively says that we have never been convicted of any crimes, in case a conviction is omitted from the background check. Mine says ‘Deightxsjre has never been convicted of any crimes’. Like you affirmatively show your GPA - which everyone has - instead of just not showing bad grades. Also like how college admissions check attendance records - which everyone has - in case the high school omits a suspension when verifying the student’s answer no - and then check the reason for absence, and if suspension, the referral. Also, all employees must have a file, even if nothing is in it.

Similarly, while I understand that someone should not be denied a job for lacking it [a woman once had an arrest report & denied job b/c no court document saying it was resolved w/o conviction b/c not charged in court], we all have a sheet titled our criminal record, and they put a black mark on it if we are convicted. A lack thereof is still a record.”.

They just looked at me again and my girlfriend rolled her eyes, and no one was angry, but they said that I was just confusing my nephew and even them by nitpicking words. Later, my girlfriend told me that I was not using proper English in my pointless explanation and said she was telling me as a friend that some people will think I am stupid for droning on like that. I have a doctorate but sometimes I am still clumsy and lack common sense and pay too much attention to details. AITA for the long explanation to my nephew?

title: AITA for not speaking to my father for over a week and storming out of the house?
text:
So my (14F) school is offering a day for students in my year group to come into the school building. My school is about 30 minutes away from my house by train. I live in London, and the UK government is easing COVID restrictions far too soon in my opinion. I am asthmatic, and I am terrified of travelling on the London Underground. Masks are compulsory, and I get claustrophobic whenever I wear one. 

Anyway, about a week ago, my dad told the school that I would be returning for that day, without telling me or asking my opinion beforehand. When he told me that he had signed me up, I was extremely upset and angry. Originally, he said that we would "play it by ear" and make a decision closer to the time. At the time, there was no information about what the day would hold, when it would be, or how long it would last. Since then, I haven't been speaking to him, apart from very basic requests or discussions about the situation. I am also very insecure at the moment, and do not feel ready to return to a place where the other students do make negative comments about your appearance. I do think I will be ready to return in September when term begins, but not at the moment.

Today, my dad told me that I was going into school whether I liked it or not, and that he was "ashamed" of the way I'd been treating him. He screamed at me to come downstairs, but I didn't want to be with people who were yelling at me in this way. He said that he didn't appreciate being ignored, and I told him that I was sick of my opinion being ignored in matters that concerned me. I then ran out of the house. AITA for reacting in this way?

side note: it's been this way for as long as I can remember. I've never been allowed to say no to things, and this isn't the first time my parents have signed me up and forced me to go to things without my prior knowledge.

title: AITA if I don't want to come to family vacations anymore?
text:
Hi,

So every summer my family organizes a family vacation in a rented house. It's usually my mom who's in charge. This year they rented a house not far from where I live, that's cool.
To add some details though : I'm not super comfortable around them I can't really explain why (one of the reasons is that we don't really share the same values and when they start joking around about subjects I'm sensible to, I don't like it. And I'm very anxious in a social setting) . Also I'm very close to one my sisters but she won't be here this year.

All that being said, that's my family, I love them and I have some really good times with them so I'm still glad to be with them for summer vacations. The thing is this year, as I said earlier, one of my sisters won't be here, and my brother probably won't be able to get here too, so there will just be me, my other sister and my parents.

The problem is : I learned through my sister (not the one who will be here) that my mom decided to invite my uncle with his 3 kids and my other sister invited a friend of her.
Now, maybe it's stupid and you'll think I'm completetly asocial but I don't really feel happy to come there anymore. I asked explanations to my mom and she told me that they just left the door open for my uncle and he can join whenever he wants so I don't really know when he will be here.

Today my dad sent me a text and I learned that way that my sister's friend came with her 2 kids as well...
So, AITA for not wanting to come there anymore? I just want to pass for 1 or 2 days but I think my mom will be sad, it's been a long time since I didn't see them. I'm telling myself that I'll see them at another moment in the month, more appropriate for me (at their home for exemple).

If you're wondering why I don't get in touch with all these informations it's just that I don't want to be on the family whatsapp, they ususally call me, text me or send a mail.

title: AITA for siding with a player in Pathfinder(Basically D&D 3.5e) as the Dungeon Master?
text:
I'm a dungeon master for a pathfinder game with 4 players, and we have played 3 sessions. The main three players for this I will call Players 1, 2, and 3.

Player 1 is playing a goblin and in pathfinder, and unless you invest points into intelligence you only speak goblin. She did not invest in intelligence, so she was unable speak common. I warned her of the problems this would cause for communication, but she said it would be fun trying to communicate with only hand signals. Player 2 put a lot of points into intelligence and learned goblin as a bonus language. As a player that can translate it can be very annoying to individually translate sentences, so the rule I implemented was that anything Player 1 says is translated unless Player 2 says otherwise.

Throughout all 3 sessions Player 2 had repeatedly refused to translate what Player 1 said on many occasions and sometimes even mistranslated it on purpose. If I had to estimate >10% of what Player 1 said got translated. It was clear that Player 1 stopped engaging with the game over the course of these sessions. 

I talked to player 1 after the first 2 sessions and she complained a bit about Player 2's method of playing. After the 3rd she said she didn’t enjoy playing with Player 2’s character and that it was ruining the game for her. I talked to her for a while, even having Player 3 chime in on her side. I ended up just helping her readjust her stats to get common as a language so she wouldn’t have to go through Player 2’s char.

Now this probably would fix the problem on its own, but I decided to talk to Player 2 about it so no further issues would happen. I asked them why they refused to translate Player 1’s statements and they talked about how their character had no obligation to translate for Player 1, and because they had nothing to gain, they didn’t. They also said its Player 1’s fault for not picking common. 

My argument against this was that while it is Player 1’s fault for not taking common, it was a dick move to not translate anything she said while having the option to. I also said that while Player 1 knew she wouldn’t be able to communicate with the party going in, having someone choose not to let her still probably felt aggravating and made her feel unable to be part of the game. 

Player 2 and me argued in circles with them calling me an asshole for bringing this up and siding with Player 1. 

I think giving common to Player 1 will fix the issue, but I felt Player 2 was an asshole about it and worry about future complications.

title: AITA for going to a hair salon that screwed over my best friend?
text:
Quick history: I live in a smaller city with only a handful of decent hair salons. I went to this salon years ago before my friend was even a hair stylist. 

For the past 4 years, my best friend has done my hair (cur, colour etc.), ever since she completed her hair school. I’ve followed her salon to salon, or she’s done it at her home. 3 weeks ago I paid her to highlight and trim my hair for an upcoming wedding of a family member I was attending. She has since cancelled numerous times on me. The most recent was 1 week prior to me flying out. The day she was meant to do my hair, she just disappeared. No texts, calls, etc. Just wouldn’t answer anything. I didn’t hear from her until 3 days later saying she was upset about her personal life and just didn’t want to do hair, and went out of town. 

This gave me 3 days to try to find a hair appointment. I called 5 salons. Finally got an appointment at her most recent salon that she quit from 2 weeks ago. Her boss was truly awful to her. He was a terrible employer, and she quit after a huge incident where he screamed at her, called her names, and blamed her for a number of things that weren’t her fault. 

I told her I went to the salon, and saw a friend of hers there because I couldn’t get into anywhere else and she flew off the handle telling me I am a terrible person for going there and supporting the owner after what he did to her. Her words were pretty forceful and she says she’s never speaking to me again. 

Granted, I understand why she’d be unhappy that my money is supporting a man who treated her so unfairly, but I was left in tight spot to find an appointment, and I didn’t mean to be disrespectful to her by making any sort of statement. 

Am I in the wrong here? I apologized and said she was right, I shouldn’t have gone, but I feel like it wasn’t the worst thing ever.

title: AITA for demanding half of the money I was promised for an incomplete foot photo service?
text:
Confusing title, I know, but here’s some backstory:

I’m not new to the world of sending foot photos / other more X-Rated content online for money. It’s not a constant thing I do, but it’s helped me out with minor college expenses for these first two ish years of studying.  I loathe doing it, but I didn’t have many other choices. While a part time job would be favorable, I couldn’t get one because I have to watch over my kid sisters most of the time. I’ve been gunning for this one job that offers flexible scheduling that would fit with my sisters schedule. 

Anyways, I haven’t done this for about half a year now, but as a midterm for my architecture major, I have to do a 3D model and need a ton of materials. I figured I would test my luck to sell some foot photos again and not have to stress my mom out about buying the photos

After oceans of bots I came across J, someone who had a good track record for paying girls. The deal was I would send him photos and videos of my feet for $80 until he finished. Half way through our session I get a call from the job I’ve been going for! After applying countless times, I was asked if I could come in for an interview later that day. I apologized to the guy, explained the situation, and began getting ready. Good news: I got the job! Upon my return I explained that I was no longer going to sell photos of myself for money, but because he had received DOZENS of photos and videos of me, I deserved at least half of what he promised. This was about a week ago, and I’ve been fighting with this guy ever since to send me the money I deserve

My girlfriend says I’m NTA. That I deserve half 
But my close friend told me that I shouldn’t be demanding money if I didn’t provide the full service. That it is what it is and that I’m TA for being so insistent on it 

AITA?

EDIT: While I didn’t provide the full service (he didn’t cum), he does have dozens of pics and vids of me saved that he ultimately isn’t paying me for

title: AITA for telling my parents not to visit?
text:

Onto the current issue. My husband has been away for work pretty much all of 2019 so far. I think we get a total of 4 weeks together as a family this summer before he leaves again. I finally put my foot down and said it would not work. 

They’re mad and upset and I should appreciate them and look at their trips as a blessing. I just want time with my family and not be stressed with their visit.  So AITA?

title: AITA For lying about my income and not telling my family I’m moving out?
text:
I (22f) still live at home with my family, and have been working for about 2 years now. When I say living with my family, I mean my ENTIRE family....mom, dad, two older sisters, my sister’s husbands, and their two children. 
One of my sisters has been acting pretty entitled lately, basically coming off as thinking that my family (especially our retired parents) should help her with her children because “that’s just what family does”. 

I had began working while also attending a community college, not really doing much with my education. I was constantly being asked for money by not just my entitled sister, but also my parents who were far into their savings trying to support us. I didn’t mind giving my parents extra (over the monthly rent I was already paying) but I hated giving my sister money. She had taken advantage of me in many situations, even stealing my identity at one point, but I loved my nieces and kept giving her money to make sure they were taken care of. 

Well this is where my asshole part kicks in. 

Last year I dropped out of my community college. I didn’t tell anyone, including my parents. And before anyone questions, I was the one paying for all of my school requirements so they didn’t have any money invested. I immediately got my job to be full time, and was working basically every day. I’ve been lying to family though, saying that I’ll be going to a class when actually I’m just going into work. Ive ended up making a much higher salary than my family has any idea about. I don’t want to be a stress to my parents, especially when I know how financially trying times are for them. I’ve saved up enough money that I’ll be able to move out to an apt with my friend by the end of this year. 

The reason I feel like an asshole is that there have been times over this year that I’ve decided to save my money where I possibly could have been helping my family by just giving that money to them. So many times my sister has asked me for money and I tell her “no”, which I’m turn leads to her just getting it from my parents. I haven’t told my family about my plans to move out, and when I do my parents aren’t going to be receiving the little money they actually get from me paying for rent, because neither of my older sisters pay them. It’ll be a significant decline in their monthly allowance when I move, and I won’t be able to be financially stable to give my parents the money they need for a good couple of months. I know in this time my sister will be a raging b**** towards them because of this. It makes me feel awful because I feel like I’m abandoning people I love on a sinking ship, but they don’t even know the ship is sinking. I just don’t know how to tell them though. 

TLDR: Living with entire family and planning to move out without telling even though one of main supporters.

Edit: Wow I wasn’t expecting this much support. I thought this would just get lost. But thank you everyone for given me advice on this. I decided I will tell my parents, on the condition they don’t tell my sisters. If the deal is broken I’ll move out sooner than I have planned for. Thank you guys again! :)

title: AITA for wanting to hurt my mom?
WIBTA if I told my MIL that she messed up with her gift?
text:
Let me start off by saying that my MIL adores me so I'm lucky in that aspect. The problem is that she's a hot mess of a person. She will call people by an incorrect nicknames FOR YEARS despite being told over and over that is 1) not a nickname for my name and 2) I hate it. My husband (her son) still tells her every time they talk that he doesn't want to be called by a diminutive version of his name. I understand that is how she is, but it still upsets me. 

Now, the issue at hand is that a few days ago, a package from her arrived. It contained the kids' Christmas gifts and a birthday present for one of them. But the present was for the wrong child! And to make matters worse, it was an ID bracelet with her name spelled wrong. I will admit that the name is a bit of an uncommon spelling but that is because we changed it so that it would match my MIL's name! 

I want to tell my MIL that the she got a gift for the wrong grandchild and that the bracelet doesn't have the correct spelling but I'm afraid that would just be causing conflict for no reason. My husband thinks I should let it go because "it's just how she is."  I'm just really annoyed by this and maybe I'm overreacting. So WIBTA if I talked to my MIL and explain that she made some mistakes with her gift?

TLDR: MIL sent a birthday gift to the wrong grandchild and didn't manage to get the name spelled correctly. I want to say something to her, but I'm afraid that would just be me looking to create drama.

title: AITA for crying in front of my husband?
text:
My  husband was diagnosed with Fetal Alcohol Syndrome at a very young age.  His birth mother was extremely young and gave him up for adoption. His  adopted parents noticed cognitive/behavioral issues from the time he was  a toddler and he was subsequently taken to multiple doctors. Once he  was diagnosed with FAS, his parents were told that he would not be able  to hold down a job, get married, or even live independently. They were  also told that he would not be able to empathize with others. His  parents are wonderful people and put an incredible amount of time/effort  in to make sure he had the best education and emotional support  possible. Fast forward to today – my husband has beaten the odds. He’s a  successful manager in his field, we have a nice house and we’ve been  married for years. He’s an intelligent, caring person and I’m extremely  fortunate to have married my best friend.

I  was raised by my mentally ill single mother. She was both verbally and  physically abusive. I spent a lot of my childhood fearing for my life,  and without a father or any siblings, I had no one to turn to. I would  spend a lot of time crying and trying to cope with my situation. I have  put a lot of effort into not becoming a rageaholic like my mother, so  when I feel overwhelmed or upset, I tend to cry rather than become angry  or raise my voice. This has repeatedly caused issues in my marriage. My  husband gets irritated/angry when I cry. At first, I tried to explain  that I wasn’t crying to upset him, but rather because it’s therapeutic  for me to cry sometimes, and it helps me not to bottle my feelings up.  This did not seem to help at all. I used to cry when we fought, but  after seeing how angry he became when I cried, I make a conscious effort  not to cry during fights. When we first got married, I was assuming my  husband would actually want to comfort me when I cried, which, I admit,  is what I became accustomed to in past relationships, particularly since  I comfort others when they’re upset. I’ve since given up on the idea  that he will comfort me, but I am in fact told to stop crying as soon as  I start. He says to stop crying, it’s not necessary, and that if it  doesn’t stop, one of us needs to leave the room. This is really hard for  me to hear, especially since it’s during a vulnerable time. He’s a  great, thoughtful person and makes an effort to tell me he loves me  often. I know this my flaw, but I do not feel loved when I can’t cry in  my own house without an argument. I also worry for the future,  particularly if I get pregnant. I am scared that I will be crying more  often and I am not sure how he will react to that. He’s beaten the odds  that were stacked against him to an incredible degree, but he has  admitted he feels zero sympathy for people who are crying, including me.

I feel like I have a right to cry in my own house without protest, despite the fact that my husband gets angry when I do. AITA?

title: AITA for sharing my roommate's gmail address with our new roommate?
text:
I live in a rented 3 bedroom house. 

With 1 room vacant, the 2 of us have been looking for a 3rd roommate via online posting boards. I have been handling the emails. 

Just did facetime interviews (limit traffic in house due to covid) to find a 3rd person. We found someone that my roommate is extremely enthusiastic about; in the call we got along well and he said he's be happy to join if we decided that. But we finished off all the subsequent booked interviews anyway. 

Decision made, I emailed the potential new guy to invite him. I cc'd the existing roommate so they would be in touch with each other and I wouldn't have some weird control over contact info. 

Existing roommate tells me it was totally inappropriate to cc him; I should have bcc'd him because I have now disclosed his personal information. I admit that I probably should have made sure it was OK but he is extremely offended saying that I am a "crazy person" for making this choice which is totally outside the bounds of normal human behavior.  He sort of implied that he would probably never have given this guy his gmail address but rather would have given him a less personal means of contact: his phone number. 

I tried explaining to him reasons they might like to be in touch with each other sooner than later and he stormed out of the room saying I am "over thinking". 

I think that many (admittedly, not all) people would be OK with this kind of sharing.  Existing roommate contends that *nobody* would ever be OK with this. 

We are all guys in our 30s.

title: AITA for getting tattoos after promising my mom I wouldn't?
text:
We are Japanese/Korean and I feel in general Asians are typically against tattoos. My family fits right in that category and is not a fan of them but I didn't find out just how strongly my mom was against it until after I got my first.

I got my first tattoo with my mom when I was 17. I got a few butterflies on my shoulder, and she got the same on her right butt cheek. She got it there because she didn't want my grandma to ever find out about it. My grandma actually found out a couple months later at the sauna and she gave disapproving eyes but didn't make anything of it. This whole matching tattoo was actually my moms idea that she brought up randomly when we were hanging out. Right after we got our tattoos, before we even left the shop, she made me promise to never get any more tattoos. I was hesitant but I agreed just to get her off my back.

Anyway, I'm 26 now with quite a few more tattoos that I've gotten since moving out many years ago. I feel really scummy about it but I believe it's my body and I have the final say with what I do with it, despite our promise. Every single tattoo I've gotten since then I've told my mom about my plans first in person because I don't want my mom to find out randomly and be surprised. All my tattoos so far have so much personal meaning to me so it hurts knowing she hates them. 

Every single time I plan on discussing with my mom about future tattoos my heart sinks to my stomach and I feel absolutely sick and anxious beforehand because I know how she'll react. She tells me every time "You promised me! Oh my gosh, you're gonna be one of THOSE people covered in tattoos" with such disappointment in her eyes then she'll give me the silent treatment for a while.

Earlier today I told her about my plans to get a matching tattoo with my husband next month and she was more upset than she's ever been, probably because all my other tattoos are easily hide-able and this would be on my forearm. She asked me how I can keep betraying her and intentionally hurting her. How would I feel if my son purposefully went out of his way to hurt and disobey me?

Obviously I would be absolutely devastated if my son intentionally hurt me in the future (he's only 2 and a half now). I don't feel this is a situation where I'm trying to intentionally hurt her though, but I know she see's it that way.

I don't think it was a fair promise to make at such a young age and about my body no less, but I still feel like such a asshole each and every time I break it. I don't know. AITA?

title: AITA For Disliking My Friend
text:
I (17F) met "Lila" (17F) in grade 7 but it wasnt until towards the end of that year that we became friends. In grade 8, she would talk about about her various interests and send me clips of the fandoms that she's into. At the end of grade 8, we lost touch due to going off to different high schools and not having my social media anymore.

A year ago, we reconnected. This time, I feel different about her. I noticed how she constantly only talked about herself and didn't really include my own interests. She would only ask about me whenerver it was related to her fandoms (eg what do you think of x? did you like them? etc). Whenever i tried to bring up my own interest, she would touch upon it and then immediately switch back to her own fandoms. Hell, a few months ago, I sent a selfie of my bestie and I (Lila is also friends with her) who we havent seen in a long time, and Lila asked a question or 2 about our mutual friend before started to talk about her interest. She has ADHD, and I'm an autistic, so I get the need to share hyperfixations to a person you trust, but I feel that the convos are one-sided most of the time.

Another thing is that she tends to vent to me a lot about her family issues, and asks me for advice. I like to help others and I'm a good listener, but I'm not good at comforting ppl, let alone give advice. A lot of times, I have to google what I should tell her in order to help her out. I've given a lot of my advice and Idk how to help without feeling like I sound like a broken record. On top of that, I have my own issues to deal with (academic, family, mental health), so it gets exhausting at times.

I rlly feel bad for disliking Lila. She's been my friend for a long time and I feel like I dislike her for selfish reasons. I've tried my best to be patient with her but its hard at this point.

title: AITA for asking my co-worker what she sees in her boyfriend?
text:
I have this co-worker who's very cute and I did consider asking her out until I found out that she had a boyfriend, one also being a co-worker. This girl is REALLY attractive and I've heard her mention being the cheerleader type when she was in high school.

Her boyfriend, on the other hand, is basically Kyle's cousin Kyle from South Park. He has this very stereotypical nerd look and he's constantly complaining about his various health issues in this high-pitched, whiny, nasally voice of his. One day he's complaining about his dry skin, the next day his sciatica, and the next his digestion problems. And he also does some things that really annoy the hell out of me. He's one of those deep breathers where his breathing is noticeably loud and distracting and he chews with his mouth wide open. It's honestly really disgusting taking a break next to him and having to hear him smack his lips together nonstop.

I don't see what my female co-worker sees in him, so I asked her today. I basically just asked her "What do you see in him?" and she replied with "Excuse me?" I repeated my question and she told me that it's none of my business. She looked pissed and I even had a manager give me a warning about the conversation. I'm just curiously asking her a question and I'm just wondering if she's right to be upset with me.

title: AITA for wanting to decline third possible roommate?
text:

They’re both awesome people and super hard workers and overall we get along great, however my friend- we’ll call her R- found another potential roomate for a three bedroom apartment we found. She behaves like a little child and loves to act oblivious towards just about everything.

An example of this: I hate being touched or hugged unless I know you extremely well, and I’ve told her countless times to please not help ícele or hug me, both of which she still does and acts completely oblivious whenever I blow a fuse at her. Besides the physical aspect I also have a really big issue with Christianity as I grew up in a cult that forced that religion on me, and I mention this because H is OKAY EXTREMELY Christian and is always trying to talk about god and shit with me- I have told her many times I have no care for her religion. She can be very snarky about it to, telling me I would be happier and healthier if I went to church, etc etc. I just see a big ol recipe for disaster with her.

title: AITA for driving into a tree?
text:
Backstory: I am an 18 year old boy who has family problems, my mother has ALS which means my dad is taking care of her all the time, so I have never been able to drive with my dad. I drove with him for like, 30 minutes.

That also means I didn't start driving until I was 18 last year in November. And I was having a blast! I was nervous at first, but that’s normal for students.

I did have a break from driving when Christmas came and didn't drive for a few weeks as lessons didn't line up, so it was safe to say, I was rusty with driving. (This is important.)

Now for the story, My driving instructor who will now be referred to as DI, met a few weeks ago and nothing has really gone wrong! A few hiccups here and there with turn signals and whatnot, but that’s expected, I'm new.

I was so afraid of crashing or doing something wrong to damage the car, I am a person who overuses the word "Sorry". I mean, I don't want to get in trouble, that's the last thing I want to happen!
DI Said something to me during lessons, "Don't worry, this car is BUILT FOR MISTAKES." This quote burned into my mind that "Oh, I'm new, small mistakes shouldn't matter much because the car is cheap and old." Which is true, the car looked really old.


Cue today, and they arrive 10 minutes early, so I quickly get off and rush outside, get inside, and try to pull out of my driveway.

There was a problem though, my dad's car was in the middle of it and when I try to drive out, I get into a bad position, so I think "Okay, I'll drive just a little forward to try to get out of here" noticing the tree in front of me, so I let off the brake and the car doesn't move forward automatically because of the snow on the ground, okay... so I press on the gas lightly... at least I try to.

I hear the engine go loud and move my foot to the brake, or at least... I thought I did.

I drove right into the tree.

Mind me, I didn't drive too fast, I don't even think I damaged the car THAT much, but before I could even say a word, before I could say sorry and apologize, DI immediately looks to me

DI: "Are you a fucking idiot!?"

Now this... this hurt me HARD instantly, at that moment my mind tries to justify me doing this, and I pipe out

Me: "B-But I didn't mean to-"

DI: "Get out."

We bicker back and forth, me trying to apologize and stay in the lesson, as I want to take the lesson! he kept on pushing for me to get out and when I still protested, apologizing more, he yells out

DI: "Get out now or I will DRAG you out!"

I picked up my stuff and got out, storming inside in shock and a bit of rage as I tell my dad what happened, and he is in shock that I well, drove into a tree...

He tries to tell me why he would kick me out of the car and everything, saying he would do it too, and I just broke into tears, I hated myself for driving into the tree and cried for 10 minutes straight, saying sorry to dad and everything...

I just stand there, crying my eyes out, only calming down 10 minutes later…

AITA?

Edit: There was someone else in the car than the DI, who is unrelated to the story, it was another student. how our driving lessons work is that either a DI or a student drives into your home, and if there is a student after your lesson you drive to their house to pick them up, and said student drives you home.

I was in a little hurry as i didn't want to keep them waiting too long.

Edit 2: I didn't tell my dad the whole story and left out the idiot part, he is reporting the DI for what he said to me and is trying to sort everything out...

I really don't want lessons to stop because of this bullshit.

title: AITA for not going through with this Craigslist transaction?
text:
I posted a very nice DSLR camera on Craigslist about a week ago. It was in perfect condition with all the original packaging, and came with some extra accessories, too. I thought the price was very fair, about 15% off from the price of the camera alone.

I got a message from an interested lady who asked me a few questions about it, then requested to meet up. She lived about two hours away from where I am, so I agreed to meet her in the middle. 

I drove for about an hour and met her outside a police station. She inspected the camera, was happy with it, and when we were about to close the deal, she tried to haggle the price with me. It wasn't a bad offer, per se, about $75 off from my original price, but that irritated me for several reasons. One, she had never once mentioned the price throughout our numerous texts, and I'd also driven an hour out of my way to meet her as a courtesy. I told her no, that the price was firm, and she could either pay the full amount or I was leaving.

She got extremely angry and told me I'm an asshole, her offer was a good offer, etc. I repeated that I was leaving if she didn't have the full amount, and she told me to fuck off and keep the camera.

Was I wrong for not accepting her offer on the spot? If she had brought it up in the beginning, I honestly might have, but I feel like you should hammer out all the details of a transaction before you agree to meet. Since she never once brought up an issue with my price or provided a counter-offer, I assumed we were good to go. It just pissed me off that I went out of my way to meet her halfway, then she had the balls to argue price with me. AITA here?

title: AITA for not wanting to postpone my Christmas dinner in order to have dinner at my in-laws?
text:
Little background I see my in-laws regularly and like them for the most part. We have seen them enough over the past few months that they are in our bubble of people we see during COVID, we are practically neighbors. 

We have already had a holiday celebration with them and planned on having a lowkey Christmas; PJ's at home watching the Star Wars movies. My husband thinks we should go this his parents and break our Christmas celebration into two days. I am annoyed that both my husband and I are finding out about this now and that it changes my plans for Christmas. This will be the first year where we haven't had to spend half the day driving and I was looking forward to spending a fun day at home. 

I want us to go over there for an hour or two in the afternoon like we originally planned but my husband thinks I am being a jerk for turning down dinner. AITA?

title: AITA for going with another friend to an event instead the group that originally invited me?
text:
So a 2 years ago (before the pandemic) I was invited to an amusement park event by some friends.  Now they’re inviting me back, but they’re also trying to invite someone who was a real dick to me.  I can be cordial around this person (I’ll call them N) but I’ve made it very clear I want them to leave me alone.  They lied to me purposefully to take advantage of me and my friends are very aware of this.  But that’s besides the point of the post.  Now I have the opportunity to possibly go with a new friend I made (I’ll call her A).  She loves amusement parks just as much as I do and I think it would be really fun.  Not only that, but I wouldn’t have to deal with being around N and having that sick feeling in my stomach.  I want my group of friends to have fun with N but I also want to have fun myself.  I can’t really do that around people I know purposefully deceived me for their own benefit.  I would really like to go with A who I trust and I know I would have a blast with.  I just don’t want to be an asshole to my other friends.

EDIT:  After some consideration and reading these comments, I officially let my friend know I was not comfortable going with N.  I told her I wish her group and N the best and a really fun night but also that it was probably best for me to remove myself from the group because of what N had done.  Now my friend is leaving me on read.

On the bright side, I was talking with A and she was ecstatic because she had never been to this event before.  We’re still finalizing plans but overall I think this event is going to be really fun with her.  I haven’t really gotten to make plans with her before so I’m super excited myself!  I don’t know if I upset my other friends or not, but as long as I’m not being an asshole then it doesn’t matter.  I’m not gonna let that ruin my fun with A.

title: AITA for taking a neutral stance in a friends fight?
text:
This happened 2 years ago. I recently remembered the incident and decided to post it here. English is not my first language so sorry for grammar mistakes.

Back then me (21m) and friend A (24 m) were sharing an apartment. Friend B (22 m) needed a place to crash for 2 weeks so we let him in. We didn't expected him to pay for his stay and refused when he offered, but he bought pizza and beer few times to make it even.

So one night we decided to have a drink in the kitchen and  B brought his laptop to put some music while we drink. When we were still sober i told them to take the laptop away since the table was unstable and there were plenty of glasses on the table. They both laughed my warning. Few hours later , when we were drunk, friend A hits the table (it was a mistake) and one lass full of beer drops on the laptop. The laptop just shutted down and wont start.  A agreed to pay reparations if they were not too much.

B took the laptop to a service and their answer was pretty devastating: too many components were damaged to make it worthed to repair, so they advised to sell the remaining good components and buy a new one. Now the laptop was not top notch, but was fairly new and expensive. Neither A and B had the money for a new one. After that they agreed to met at our apartment( B moved while we were waiting for response since his problem got solved). When i heard that they met i took my jacket and went outside. I don't wanted to be part of their negotiations and stay neutral.

Their deal was next: B buys a new laptop and they split 50-50 the price and  A gets the old laptop so he can sell the components. No one knew how much that was still worthed. The entire situation got even more complicated after, because B decided to sell  the old laptop himself and keep the money. He believed he got tricked by A into a deal that he didn't wanted ( he wanted for A to fully pay for a new similar laptop) and went with this deal because otherwise he wouldn't have gotten anything. Now A is mad as well because he didn't got the old laptop.

In the end they both believed they got tricked by the other one and I was part of it since i chosed to stay neutral.

TL;DR: AITA for being neutral in a friends fight ?

title: WIBTA if I told my parents I won't watch their dogs anymore?
text:
My parents have 2 fairly small dogs, both around 2 years old. They have had them since they were puppies. They taught them some tricks, but nothing in regards to behaviour. One of them is chilled out and is great company, but the other is a completely different story. She'll go for your food the second your back is turned, frequently does her business inside (they have a dog door) and has separation anxiety, meaning she will get quite scared if she is left alone for extended periods of time. I can't fault her for that last one, but I can't deny it makes doing lots of things challenging.

I have been working from home recently due to COVID, and during the past 2 weeks my parents have been on holiday. Today I was/am working late, so I decided to order a pizza. The pizza arrives, so I left it on a high counter in the kitchen so I could get my laptop from my desk. By the time I return, maybe 5 minutes later, the 2nd dog has somehow jumped up on the counter (everyone in my family leaves stuff there so she can't get to it) and ate a WHOLE 13.5" pizza. At first I was really mad at her, It's not often that I order takeaway. I put the few pizza crusts that were left in to the bin and went back to my desk.

After calming down (admittedly I got really mad over something so small) I decided to put her in her cage as I have heard eating pizza can cause vomiting among other things, so I wanted to minimise the blast radius. This sorta thing happens just about every time I'm not in her line of sight, and I have had to put up with it for the past two weeks. WIBTA if I told my parents I won't watch their dogs anymore? They're a package deal sorta thing on account of the aforementioned separation anxiety.

P.S. I'm the last person who will watch them, both of my sisters refuse to for similar reasons

EDIT: I have my own place, I am only at my parents' to look after their dogs/house

title: AITA for asking my boyfriend to let me share a bed with a friend while on vacation?
text:
My boyfriend (22M) and I (25F) are in a long distance relationship since April. I’m much more comfortable physically with people and think more things are platonic. Because of this there’s some things I’d do with close friends such as cuddling that he doesn’t want me to do because he thinks that’s for a partner. This has led to me just stopping doing some things because I understand boundaries are different and I want to respect his and for the most part I’ve never said anything about it. 

The other day I was talking about a trip I’m taking to see friends for the first time since 2019. We’re staying in an Airbnb and there’s the chance I may share a bed with my best friend. My boyfriend isn’t going because the borders closed and this was planned awhile ago. On almost every trip I’ve been on some people will share beds just because it’s easier to get a place with three beds versus six. I’ve never thought of anything of it, but my boyfriend said he doesn’t want me to share a bed with anyone ever that’s not him or my family. He said it’s something he views as intimate. But then later he said if I was straight he’d be okay with me sharing with female friends, I’m bisexual to clarify. So basically I can never share a bed with friends, even ones I’m very close to and not interested in, because I’m attracted to their gender. 

I tried to clarify that nothing would happen and it’d literally be me sleeping three feet away from a close friend in a bed the way it’s always been on the past trips. He said it wasn’t that he didn’t trust me or friends just that it was a thing he didn’t want me to share with others. In my opinion sharing a bed isn’t a big deal and if he went on vacation I wouldn’t even think to ask about the bed situation. Even if he was sharing with a female friend I wouldn’t mind. So I tried to offer compromise by saying things like: I’ll do my best to get an Airbnb with enough beds on vacations, if not then I’ll always sleep on a couch when I can, if I do have to share though I’ll keep it to close friends and get the biggest bed, then lastly I’ll tell him ahead of time all the details. He’d most likely be going on future trips too so we’d share a bed those times. He said this wasn’t compromise because on occasion I could have to share a bed and that’s not what he wants.  He didn’t offer compromise on his end. He said I could pay extra to get my own bed every time, but based on my experience that could be pricey such as an extra $300-$500 each time. He then said I should bring an air mattress with me, even on trips I’m flying, to ensure I don’t share a bed. 

I’m trying to respect his boundaries but I really think sharing a bed isn’t intimate at all and I don’t want to have to make special accommodations every time because of it. It’s frustrating because I feel that I tried to compromises and he refuses.

So AITA?

title: AITA For wanting to carpool with my friend, even though its illegal?

WIBTA if I pushed coming with him instead of driving by myself?

title: WIBTA if i uninvite one of my groomsmen from my wedding?
text:
Title says it all. I am getting married in May, and i have recently found out one of my groomsmen has a heroin habit he has been hiding from us. When i found out, I was shocked but asked him if he had plans to stop or seek recovery, but he seemed adamant that he does not have a problem and that he would continue to do heroin "recreationally". Unfortunately, in the multiple follow up convos I have had with him, this mindset has not changed. My fiance's brother is a recovering addict as well, which complicates matters further. 

My fiancee is entirely uncomfortable with him being there, and i honestly see why. However, i also do not want my friend to feel like i am abandoning him in a time of need. I do not think it is fair to have him at our wedding while my fiancee and I are worried he may start fiending for a hit or get my BIL involved somehow. I do not want to cut him out of my life either and will continue to support him should he choose to go sober, i just dont think being at the wedding is the best decision for him or us. So reddit, WIBTA if i uninvite him from my wedding?

title: AITA I scold an almost deaf person for caughing over 6 months
text:
Hi Reddit, This is my first post and i'm not a native, so sorry If i don't follow all the rules or speak a bad English. I live in an old house build in the 60s and i am currently living with 3 Others roommates.

Note Here, we don't really talk a lot nor spend any time together since all of us are very busy.

Now the latest roommate went in 6 month ago and he is almost deaf. He is working as a Gardner and has to get up very early for work.

My room is close to the kitchen and since its an old house, I can hear everything threw 2 closed doors.

For the Most Part my roommate is very silent but he is coughing a lot. Its very loud and he Always wakes me up.

We were having this problem for 6 months but i never said anything. But one time i was stressed and i Messages him in the morning that He is very loud and Always wakes me up. He answered immediately, that he "did nothing wrong" and "it is normal to cough all day". I told him its Not the Problem that He coughed a view times but that never did sth to cure this. I told him "If you have a cold or something you better wear a scarf and Drink Lots of water." He denied doing something wrong and insistent i shall Look for other roommates Bad Habits (because if they do something wrong, he gets a free Pass)

Lately i hear him drink more water and Sometimes even use a tissue. I think he tries something and Hope he becomes better.

Anyways i feel Kind of Bad because i scolded him regarding His own health and Body, even tho i didn't know all of His Background. But still im Happy that i can sleep in Peace now.

AITA for scolding him?

Edit: Hi guys, thanks for your honest opinion. I apologized to my roommate 3 days ago via WhatsApp, because I didn't see him ever since. He didn't reply but he is greeting me recently again.
I guess that means it wasn't a complete failure.
Also in general I think I am doing better. My various habits pushing away people for stupid reasons is decreasing. I think that has something to do with my self-confidence. I try not to be beaten by my depression. This way I got more energy to treat people around me better. Before the idea "just do what is right" was not possible, because I was not strong enough. The more I do my best, the better I feel and treat people with respect.
For anyone who has/had the same problems, feel free to pm me.
Greetings

Edit2: I fixed the obvious typos (=

title: WIBTA if I told my friend I don't care about a YouTubers relationship?
text:
On mobile so sorry for any formatting issues.


Me (26F) and my friend, let's call her Lucy (27F) have been friends for a long time, almost 10 years.

We have a lot in common and we both enjoy the same games. 

Now I like playing this game but I don't watch any YouTube videos on it because I find the community around it quite toxic and overly negative at times. I used to but I stopped because I just want to enjoy the game. 

Recently two YouTubers who play the game a lot started dating, surprising no one, and it's all my friend will talk about. How no one surprised, how obvious it was this would happen, how it's going to cause drama when they break up. 

Now I'd seen about it the day before her and I shrugged and moved on. The guy is one of the reasons I stopped watching the videos, he's so negative and I find him quite condescending and she's just too much. So when she said my reaction was, "Oh yeah, I saw that yesterday. Good for them."

But now she will not shut up about it. She goes on and on like,  "Did you see her video? She forgot about a rule and asked him about it."

Like...okay. So I responded that it was weird she would ask him and not just look it up. I mean she has a phone, multiple monitors, a printer I'm sure. She could have the rules somewhere accessible. But whatever, I don't care. 

The problem is she went on and on about it. I was giving one word answers to her paragraphs and she kept going. 

In the end I just started dropping hints that I was tired and went to bed. But I've woken up to more messages from her about them. 

WIBTA if I just told her I don't care and I don't want to hear any more about them? Like it's obviously something she's really interested in and she is my best friend but I just do not give a shit.

title: AITA for being upset that my parents bought my brother a new car with my money after a car accident that was my fault?
text:
I (18F) got into an accident and totaled my car. Keep in mind that I bought myself that car and paid for my own insurance. The accident was my fault but not entirely. I ran a stop sign that was covered by trees and hit a parked car to avoid hitting a moving one. The house owner at the corner said that I’m the fourth person to get into an accident because of the same stop sign in under 3 months. Thankfully, no one was hurt.

My insurance decided to buy off my totaled car for $2000. I gave the money to my parents to put away to get myself a new car. On top of that, I also gave them $3000 from my savings for my car (I give them my money to put away because I don’t trust myself to not spend it). My parents were livid that I got into an accident. Although nothing came out of their pockets other than my insurance rising that I pay for, they decided to punish me and use the money that my insurance gave me AND my savings to buy my brother (23M) an new car.

I told them that it was my money and that they had no right to do that. All they said was that if I hadn’t gotten into an accident, the car they bought for my brother would’ve been mine. Now my insurance is a lot more expensive than what it was before and they said that with a new car, it’s going to be more expensive. I said that it was my car that I bought and I was paying for insurance and that there was no need to punish me for something that didn’t affect them financially. They don’t care and gave the car to my brother anyway, saying this is what I get for not being careful when driving a car, I don’t get one at all.

AITA for getting upset?

EDIT on why I give my parents my money: My parents  got a safe to keep some of their money in after their account got wiped because their identity was stolen some years ago. When my brother was my age, he would give our parents his money to keep in the safe and it worked out well for him and got his money back when he asked. I thought my money would be treated the same.

title: AITA for reporting traffic noise during the stay-at-home order?
text:
It’s Friday night and I live off a highway. I’ve lived here for over 10 years so I know what it sounds like. It’s been super quiet since a stay-at-home order was put in place, but tonight it’s as loud as it’s always been. 

I sent a message to the mayor’s office but I don’t know if it was right, because part me is like, ok maybe it’s just that there’s more doctors and nurses going to the hospital, or maintenance staff that are going to work. So, there’s going to be more police presence and unnecessary pullovers. The other part of me is like, this is a bunch of people coming home from parties because it’s very late on a Friday night.

I kind of know the answer but I dunno. I don’t want people being pulled over unnecessarily, possibly making them late for work or coming home, but I also don’t want more people getting sick because there might be a bunch of parties going on.

Please help with my moral dilemma.

title: AITA for getting a shirt that says “Goddamn” on it, and that “glorifies drug use”
text:
I (19f) recently ordered a shirt because I thought it was funny. It’s a picture from Pulp Fiction of Uma Thurman in the bathroom rubbing her nose (after snorting cocaine) and it says “goddamn” on it (from the bathroom scene). I never intended on wearing it in public, I just liked the shirt (and I like the movie), so I got it to wear around the house. My parents, just now, saw it after I unwrapped the packaged in the kitchen, and they gave me endless shit for it. 

They said that it was “glorifying drug use” and “used to lords name in vain.” I told them it isn’t glorifying drug use, but instead that it was an iconic scene from a cult classic, and I even said I had no intentions on wearing it outside of the house. As for the religious part, my mother has always been really judgmental about my religious beliefs. Any time I’ve said “I don’t believe in god” she would get angry with me and tell me that it’s disrespectful. She also has always told me that one day I’ll “come around.”

I got upset when my mom told me this is the equivalent to wearing a shirt that said “fuck allah” on it, to which I said that was a dumb argument, because the shirt itself isn’t about god, but it’s a quote from a movie because “goddamn” is a common phrase used in colloquial language. She then tried to argue that it’s as bad as wearing a shirt that says “heil hitler” and I said it isn’t even close, because a shirt with a commonly used phrase on it is not the same as condoning someone who was responsible for a genocide. 

I told her it’s absurd that she would get this mad at me for getting this shirt, yet she thought it was funny when my boyfriend wore a soviet union shirt one time. I told her she’s only mad because this doesn’t fit *her* beliefs, and it stems from her obvious disappointment that I’m an atheist. 

My parents have both said they’re very disappointed in the “lack of taste and class” in this shirt, and that they “thought I was more socially aware” than this. That part is mind boggling, especially because they’re also the kind of people so say that my generation is full of oversensitive people for wanting gender neutral bathrooms, or being hyper aware of people’s preferred pronouns. I called them hypocrites and I have spoken to them since. 

Maybe the shirt is as insensitive as they say, and maybe I am an asshole. I want perspectives. AITA?
