title: AITA for complaining about cleaners and they overheard
text:
Cross-posted from embarrassing moments, and I'm on mobile so sorry for any formatting issues!

So we bought a new home in February, and we decided to get our air ducts cleaned, as the previous owners had never done so. I am working from home and had a conference call scheduled for part of the time period they gave for their window to arrive.

They came in, set up their stuff both downstairs and upstairs (where I'm at), and my fiance followed them, explaining where all the ducts were. They proceeded to stand outside the door where I'm working and try to convince him to buy special cleaning stuff for COVID-19, for an extra $300. His spiel was loud and long, while I very obviously was on a conference call (on speaker phone). Since they were being so loud, I left and went outside to finish my call.

After that finished, I went back up to my office. My fiance came up and I vented about how they knew I was in a call and yet didn't have the courtesy to go downstairs or even to another room. Fiance mentioned that they were arguing back and forth as well, and he didn't like them at all. I stated we would not use the company again and I'd send in an email to the company to give details about the experience.

That's when one of the guys popped up the stairs and into my room. He very obviously heard us speaking, as a little while later he popped in again and asked if I was in a call (obviously wasn't, as there were no sounds coming from my phone and it was nowhere remotely near me). When I said no, he commented that he didn't want to interrupt if I was on a call. When he left he said he promised her wouldn't bother me again.

So, while I'm still unhappy with how things were handled, I now feel incredibly embarrassed that they overheard my frustrations, and am now wondering if I'm TA.

title: AITA for leaving a bad, but honest review?
text:
me:  me 

the plush: PINKY

nice seller: NS 

my little brother: LB

my brutally honest mom: BHM

So today I received this cute little axolotl plush from Etsy. It was handmade by NS and shipped pretty quickly. When I got upstairs and opened the package I remarked one very noticeable detail about PINKY. His colours were totally off. Instead of being warm hot pink and a slightly darker pink around the ear thingies, the fabric was a very very pale pink (almost white) and a dark blood coloured pink around the external gills. Upon further inspection i found three grey stitches on one of his external gills, and more grey stitches on one of his legs also there was glue residue on the bottom of his eyes and a visible hole right under his tail. To be completely honest I was disappointed since it was nothing like what I expected. The design and craftsmanship was still really good. I left my review with 2 stars and an apology at the end (I know that doesn't justify it i'm sorry). She responded soon after with a full refund and an apology. I felt horrible because not only have I made her loose the money I paid (65$CAD) she also lost the money spent on the materials and wasted her time. But just to defend myself a little I expected more with the decently large amount of money I paid. I tried to take down the review after a little chat with LB and BHM to no avail because for some reason Etsy forbids the editing of any review under 3 stars. (I put 2)

me: \*explains what happened\*

BHM: you shouldn't have left a review. It would have been much better if  you discussed it with her privately so she could work out a solution with you.

me: I just feel like thats important information to have when buying a product. (not knowing that a low  amount of stars would change anything)

LB: what? what's wrong with you

BHM: Now instead of keeping it private your review is damaging her business

me: \*me feeling even more terrible trying to think of something\*

and I left to go ponder,

My idea was to buy a different plush from her with the money refunded so she could get the money back and I could leave a better review on that one. 

side not: I left NS numerous apologies asking what I could do to help. (I feel like satan)

so AITA for leaving an honest review?

title: AITA for being distant towards my parents, specifically because of my mom?
text:
This has been on my mind a lot and honestly I just need closure.

I (F15) don't show it, but I love my parents. I appreciate everything they do for me. But I really have become extremely distant with them.

My parents just are hard to be around. *Very* hard sometimes. They constantly stress me out to the point where I have actual grey hairs.

I'm getting more cold with them for various reasons.
Now the main issue is my mom. My dad of course is a huge problem too at times but, I'd rather not talk about that since it hasn't been relevant latley. But my mom is so DIFFICULT to be around. My whole life it's her fault why I've been sad or stressed. I try and tell her that she makes me feel awful but she doesn't care. At all. Now, this probably wouldn't be a big deal. But if it wasn't I wouldn't find a reason or two not to be around her. Which is stuff like:

- Being extremely strict when it comes to school. And I mean " Have a B and you're screwed" strict. Originally having one B was okay but since quarantine she took it back because I'm at home now. And school gives me huge anxiety because of her being strict. Like, I actually got a panic attack in the shower once because I IMAGINED me getting a B at school.

- Using me ( and my dad) as servants. She would ask my dad to rub her feet. If he doesn't she would WHINE until my dad did. Annoying to hear DAILY. Same with me, I say no, she whines or gets angry. And I don't mean just getting her water or something, it's bringing food to her on the couch, ice cream, stuff like that. It's ridiculous.

And RECENTLY:

- My mom didn't like how I'm on my phone a lot and told me to give my phone to her so she went to look at it. Of course I did because I have nothing to hide. I was fine until she looked through my MESSAGES and confronted me about cursing while texting my friends. Not cursing AT them, just cursing. She got so shocked and I tried to defend myself that I wasn't cursing to be insulting on purpose and that I make sure to not curse to adults at all, and cursing towards friends with no intention of using curses as insults. But no. After she was done she told me to take the password on my phone. Why? In her words " So I can look through it whenever I want at night".She works at night. While I'm asleep. So she would look through it WHEN I'M ASLEEP!! I know this because I have woken up to her trying but my phone was locked in the past. I tried to argue with her but I eventually gave up, because I didn't want her to get angry.

So obviously, I'm bitter. Towards both of them, and especially my mom as you can see. But I kinda feel like I'm the AH because they try to talk to me but I just refuse. My dad would enter my room or my mom will ask me to stay with them in the living room, but I'd just say no. I'm tired of all of this. And no matter how many times I'll talk to them, they won't change. But even so, I still feel bad.

So, AITA? If I am, I will take accountability of my actions and be the bigger person.

title: WIBTA If I let my employee(manager) go?
text:
DISCLAIMER: This is obviously a burner account as my original account is known by my friends, colleagues and so on.

So I run a business in Istanbul, Turkey which is a touristic shop that sells spices and other local products mostly to foreign and local tourists. I do not actually own the company, the original owner of it is my longtime friend and he lets me run the business for him in exchange for a rental fee. (Which is odd because under every formal document there's his signature, he signs them for me. By doing it, he relies solely on my trade because by nature I am getting in debt in the name of him.) 

So there is a guy in my shop that sells most of the products out of all the employees. I give him all kind of exceptions and try to make him happy (like last week I gave him 5 days off just because he said me he 'felt' such a vacation.) Whenever he feels ill, he comes to the shop, does what he needs to do in order to make shop ready in the first 2-3 hours and then says he feels ill and neither I nor the manager of shop pressure him, we usually let him go to hospital and generally give him an off-day without a paycut. He has been in many scuffles past 3-4 days and constantly discussed harshly with his colleagues. I've been thinking of letting him go for the past weeks as he had discussed with my manager as well, causing hierarchical problems and ego wars within the shop. 

This morning, I heard that the two have been involved in another discussion and that my manager swears on the said employee. I called them both and listened the story from both sides. They were too angry at each other that I decided it is impossible for them to work together anymore. I told my manager to go out of the office and said the other employee that we should part ways with him. He understood and said he was neither angry nor disappointed at me. He then proceeded to his pocket to show me a paper. He said that yesterday his manager wanted to send him to the bank and take a credit in the name of himself. The manager wrote a paper in the name of my childhood friend (legal owner of the shop) and signed it in the name of him.

Now I don't want to step back of my initial decision to let the employee go because he didn't tell me or show the paper to me until he was sure he was fired. I also want to penalize the manager as he embarassed me and I would be worse than dead if my friend found out about this. Now WIBTA if I let both of them go? Is there any other ways to penalize him silently without publicizing the issue because my manager and I have been working together for more than 15 years?

TLDR; am a employer, accidentaly learned my employee is getting involved in a fraud which also regards my childhood friend, WIBTA if I fire him?

title: AITA for losing my cool over rising wedding costs?
text:
Warning: Long and about wedding stuff.

My daughter is in her early 20s and getting married in a few months. She initially wanted to get married a year ago in a small intimate ceremony with 10 people. Her future MIL pitched a fit and she and fiancé settled on a 50 person wedding with an extra year before they married to make her happy. 

The original budget we gave was 5k. We did not know the going rates for some of this stuff, as hubby and I eloped. Costs have been steadily creeping up but we managed to put aside enough to cover things. In the mean time her fiancé’s sister got engaged and had a 300 person wedding that was very expensive and had a lot of extras.

My daughter staunchly said at first she didn’t want a wedding video. Photos yes, video no. Fiancé wanted the video. We love them so we are trying to give them what they want, especially as fiancé’s family have not been the most supportive.

We come to today. I was working a temp job, hoping it would become permanent. All the while, I put about 25% of my pay into wedding fund and hubby’s overtime is going in there as well. Today I got let go from the job. I had already told my daughter this before the incident happened. She calls me later in the evening gushing about this videographer she found who is going to be doing a fancy set up and how happy she is. The cost is only $1200. I couldn’t help myself. I asked she take me off speakerphone and then I told her how frustrated I am with the ballooning wedding costs. I wanted to know if this is what she really wanted. She mentions that after seeing fiancé’s sister’s wedding a month ago she really wants this video thing to happen. I snapped. I mentioned that the only reason we were spending all this money was because fiancé’s mom threw a fit and now hubby and I keep having to pay more and more. To make it worse, because fiancé’s parents spent all this money on their daughter’s wedding a month ago, I am having to spend even more. This isn’t the first time she has gone from “I just want this decor/dress/event to be very simple” to “I think I like this fancier thing better and really want it.”

Any other time I would have kept my cool. She knew I just lost my source of income and I am stressed because it took me a while to get that job. I had been busting my butt doing gig work while looking for a job in my degree field after getting my degree in December.

AITA for letting her know how upset I am at the continually rising costs and making her feel guilty? (I tried not to, but I totally ended up crying during the call.)

UPDATE: I spoke with my daughter this morning. We are giving her the rest of the money we have in her account and she can use that to pay for the expenses. We agreed that any shortfalls from here out were for her and fiancé’s family to cover. She apologized for her behavior and said that she understood my frustrations. She explained the situation to her fiancé and he is also on board with the new financial arrangement. She plans to call and chat with me again after her shift at work today, so it seems that we have moved forward from this. We both said we were determined not to let this wedding spoil our relationship and to keep communicating with each other.

title: AITA that I didn’t notice my friends were missing from my sc?
text:
I’m a 17 f. 

So two weeks ago Snapchat deleted everyone’s private stories and then gave them back the same day. I guess doing that took some people out my private story but I didn’t notice until today. I added back who I could but I couldn’t remember all. Apparently among the 10 or so people removed, two of my close friends were taken out and I never knew. I texted one friend today (Sarah) just to chat and she opened my message and didn’t say anything. I was confused so I texted her again and she said that she and the other friend (Taylor) had been purposely ignoring me because they thought that I took them and only them out on purpose. I was legitimately confused because I didn’t do that at all, Snapchat did. My private story isn’t even the place where I bare my soul it’s just a place for funny memes. She explained how they felt that I had excluded them for a reason and that they were hurt by it. I explained to her what happened and she said she just said ok. 

There was no way I could’ve noticed sooner because when I post I don’t look at who viewed it and even if I did view it there’s 50+ people in my private story so how could I single out who saw it daily ? 

This has actually happened before, but last time it was a 2nd private story with just 6 or so people inside and I purposely didn’t put them in that one because it was about my relationships and they had made it clear they weren’t interested in that side of my life and judged me often for it. When I added them in the story they barely interacted and would use some of the posts to make jokes at my expense, but I still kept them in my private story. 

I was also taken out of people’s private stories but didn’t bother to ask to be put back in because it wasn’t that important to me.

I feel kind of hurt as well because I have trying to contact both for weeks and they have both ignored me and blew me off instead of talking to me about it, it gave me anxiety because I thought that they were cutting me off. Turns out I was right 🤷🏾‍♀️, I’m sad cause Sarah made me feel like I ruined my friendship with them but idk. I’ve been crying for a while now because I now I feel like a terrible person in their eyes. 

Just wanted to know, AITA? 


*Sorry if it’s kind of all over the place, I’ll answer any questions for clarity*

title: AITA for not telling my boss about internal job application in another department
AITA for being mad at my friends
text:
On Thursday night, I (19 F if it matters) asked my friend group if they wanted to play some online games together via Zoom; 4 of them said yes, and we agreed on Saturday 7pm. At 7:05, nobody joined. I asked if anyone was coming, and some said they couldn’t because they were eating / needed to do HW. I waited for a few more minutes, and seeing that nobody joined, texted the group that I'm signing off on Zoom, we can do this at a different time if they still want, and wished them great luck on the upcoming quarter.

I muted the chat notifications and started crying. I really missed my friends, and put in lots of research to see what online games would be fun for us play. I felt really unwanted, and started believing that my friends were just using excuses to brush me off ( we already decided on 7 so they should have eaten earlier; it's only the first week of school and there's not much if any homework; etc), which now that I think about it, is probably not true. Nevertheless, at that point, I resented myself for pretending everything is okay, and for constantly being a people-pleaser, even after all my friends just ditched me.

Anyway, I opened the chat 2 hours later, and saw that one of my friends said she was talking to her parents and therefore couldn't come. This time I wanted to say my feelings, but in a way that wouldn't  make the situation too intense. I ended up sending these exact texts: "No worries, just disappointed 🧐(thought emoji)", followed immediately by "^ I'm joking, just to make it clear". 

None of my friends said anything. I reviewed my text. Obviously now that I calmed down a bit, it came off as a bit harsh, maybe passive aggressive, and possibly a bit entitled. Right now, I'm a bit torn between just leaving it like this and apologizing to my friends for what I said. Redditors, AITA?

title: WIBTA if i asked someone else to give me away?
text:
throwaway because some of my family use this.

so i (28f) am planning my wedding with my fiancé (36m). i have asked his advice on this but he just wants me to do what i think will be good for me.

i have never really seen eye-to-eye with my parents. i have always done the opposite of what they wanted to make myself happy (dressed the way i wanted, dated who i wanted, etc. i have two older siblings (40f and 43m) and they have always been the ‘golden children’.

i am well aware of the tradition of having your father walk you down the aisle but i just don’t know if i could bring myself to ask him after everything we’ve been through. 

now i have plenty of other options in mind for who i would ask but i know that my family would have a meltdown if i asked anyone other than my father. 
like i said before, my fiancé wants me to do what’s best for me and he supports me 100%. 

i want my family there but i don’t want them to have any official roles (if that makes sense), my nieces and nephews will because they have done absolutely nothing wrong and i love them too pieces. 

i’m at a loss at what to do.

title: AITA for telling my roommate that she lost her chance and should move on?
text:
I (31f) live with two other girls - Other Roommate (22f) and Lovelorn (23f). They are a good deal younger than me which is why sometimes I can’t take them too seriously. They are also good friends whereas I am a stranger. It’s important to know that we share a backyard outside space with another house where Boy Next Door(25m) and his two roommates live. 

When I first moved in, the outdoor space was basically just a pile of dirt and mud and it stayed that way when Lovelorn and Other Roommate came to live there. When Boy Next Door moved in though, he began gardening, planting stuff, etc. I thought it was pointless at first but it actually became kind of nice after a lot of work but he had only improved his house’s “half” of the backyard. Lovelorn got interested in what he was doing and the two started hanging out and he showed her some gardening tips so that she could do the same to our half which I thought was nice.

He eventually asked her out on a date but Other Roommate told her that she shouldn’t say yes right away. I’ll also admit that I discouraged it because if things turned awkward between them, it could easily turn things awkward between the two houses. 

She eventually said no and he moved on though for a while I think they still hung out. There were a lot more group hangs between him, my two roommates and his then one-on-one hangs. I’m not sure I approve personally because of COVID but I guess we’re all in the same bubble. Anyways, aparently, as of two weeks ago, he officially has a girlfriend and Lovelorn is furious. 

She’s been moping around the house refusing to go into the backyard area that she helped make so nice “because it reminds me of him.” She’s even claimed recently that he was the one which I think is ridiculous. She even claims that his girlfriend isn’t good enough for him despite never having met her. 

She’s become more of a mess and it shows in the house (dirty dishes everywhere, old clothes left around the house, etc). I’m not unsympathetic to heartbreak but he was just one guy. I told her that she shouldn’t have played games, accepted when he asked her out and since she didn’t she should move on. 

Now Other Roommate and Lovelorn are calling me a heartless bitch and things have gotten incredibly tense here. AITA?

title: AITAH For being mad at my mum for expecting me to pay for my own leavers jumper
text:
First time posting 
Okay so I'm 16f, I've just left school as that's the age you leave school in the UK. I do have a job but however I don't have enough money to pay for it all on my own otherwise I would ,so I asked my mum if she could help me out, and she got annoyed with me. I get that she's got things to pay for between her and my dad but I just thought maybe since they paid for my sister's jumpers that maybe they'd help me out , I don't ask for much because I'm quite independent both financially and with my day to day life, my sister said I'm being abit of an asshole but I just want others opinion. 
EDIT:
The Jumper itself is £21 , with an added £3 if I want a nickname on it , I said to my mum "I can pay for half of it with the extra for the nickname"  
So Reddit AITA here
UPDATE: 
So I managed to budget my money for the rest of the month until pay day to afford my levers hoodie. This was including my nickname on the jumper and delivery was free thankfully, anywho  I haven't spoken to my mum about it since this morning and she also doesn't know that I've bought it.

title: AITA for not paying the full rent and for sticking up for my friend?
text:
I was in a flat on my own at 20, I had this neighbour and after getting to know him one day he started doing heroin in my flat after he left, I rang up my friend and was crying to him, he spoke with his mum and told me to pack everything and that I could move in that same night. 

So after moving in with my friend and his family I was told by my friend's sister’s boyfriend that I wouldn't need to pay any rent and I should save it up so I could move out quicker, I stayed for a few months, my friends mum would buy me takeaways almost every night but it became near Christmas and they wanted me out before then so I was looking for somewhere to live with the money I had saved while living there so I could move out then about a week later my friend's sister’s boyfriend told me I had to pay rent, I asked how much she wanted and he said £50 which doesn't seem a lot but now I was £50 short from moving out, I phoned my mum, and told her what happened and she got me a new flat, I should have done that at my old flat but things just happened so fast.

5 years later my friend and his brother wanted to come round to have a few drinks I was skeptical because I’m not a drinker, my friend told me  how much alcohol he had, around £300 worth after a bit of convincing I said they could come round, after a few hours, we all got drunk had a laugh etc, then my friends brother started asking for me to pay some money towards the alcohol, he wanted me to pay half but I had only drunk about £20 worth but I was manipulated into paying the full £150 because they brought up the whole incident above ,so I ended up paying the money and we had a good time after that so I asked if I could invite a friend round and they said sure, so I rang him up and he came round, immediately after he came round my friends brother asked him for £40 to put towards the alcohol, he didn't have any money with him because he assumed it would be free as friends usually do, I told them to take his share out of the money I had already paid since I didn't drink anywhere near that amount but they demanded that my friend pay, my friend said "I’ll leave" I saw him out and apologized to him outside, I came back inside and both of them started arguing with me saying i was putting friends before family I said friend did nothing wrong and they were out of order which made them angry and they brought up the above again, I explained that my friends sister’s boyfriend told me I didn't have to pay rent at which point that caused an argument, i told them to get out my flat and they refused to leave and  I had to resort in threatening to call the police if they didn't leave which only made things worse, they blackmailed me into paying them another £100 for them to leave because I didn't want to call the police on family, they eventually left after me paying them the £100.

so, am I the arsehole for not paying the full rent and for sticking up for my friend that left instead of family?

title: AITA For telling my sister she can’t visit me at college?
text:
I (20f) invited my sister (18f) to come up and visit me at college about a month ago. We decided that this upcoming weekend (my birthday) would be the best, and were planning on going to a football game. She also wanted to invite her friend (18f) because she didn’t want to drive up (2.5hours) alone. I’ve met this friend a few times but am not super close or good friends with her at all, but she always seemed nice so I thought it was fine. Her and my sister both scheduled off work for that weekend (they both work minimum wage, part time jobs).

I had told my sister to come up Friday night or Saturday, since I have classes till 4:30 on Friday and wouldn’t be able to hang out during the day. She seemed to agree with this. About 3 weeks ago, I tried to buy tickets to the game but they sold out. I let her know immediately, and said that I could buy tickets for next weeks game or another game, but she said no because her and her friend had already requested off work for the weekend we planned. I told her she could just request another week instead and she said she didn’t want to. So, we planned on just tailgating the game and still hanging out and me showing her around town.

Well, a few nights ago I asked her when she’s planning on coming down, and she tells me they’re going to leave at 7am Friday morning (so arrive at about 9:30am). I reminded her I had classes all day till 4:30 and wouldn’t be able to hang out. She said that’s fine, and that they were going to drive to the other college in the state (an additional 2 hours away from me) to visit her friend’s friends. Now also keep in mind I’m lending her my car for this trip bc she wrecked hers  a month ago. She also informed me her and her friend are going out partying that Friday night, which she knows I don’t like (she also doesn’t know anyone else at the college, so not very safe). She also then informed me her friend got put on the schedule for Saturday (and her friend didn’t want to tell them no), so they’d have to leave at noon. I told her that’s way before the tailgating starts and most shops and places don’t open till 10am on saturdays, so we would mainly just be sitting at home and not be able to do anything. She said she doesn’t care bc we’re not actually going to the game anyway.

This is where I might be TA, bc I told her if she wants to go on a trip, go on a trip, just don’t use visiting me as an excuse. She hasn’t said anything to me since. AITA?

title: AITA for “parenting” another person’s kid?
text: I [29F] was at the park with my 3 year old daughter when I noticed that a kid on the playground had tripped and was crying out for their mother. This had gone on for a few minutes when my motherly instincts kicked in and ran over with a band-aid and put it on the kid where she had a tiny scrape. I noticed that 2 parents, who had previously been on their phones not paying attention to their kid, immediately ran over after I had put the band-aid on their kid, fuming with rage that I was “parenting” their kid. They were saying things like not to touch their child since I was a stranger, etc. I told them that I just noticed the child was hurt and wanted to help. AITA for giving this kid a band-aid?

title: WIBTA for threatening to throw people out of my wedding for discussing politics?
text:
(Mobile)

Me and my fiancee are getting married this Nobember, which is right in the middle of U.S. election season. title: AITA for essentially not wanting to break the law and lie?
text:
Sorry in advance for being vague. 

So I work in a specific Industry with very strict rules and regulations. There are severe fines for non compliance and just generally misinforming us or trying to work around your responsibilities. 

My partner and I are discussing doing something with our property and he wants to not just skirt the rules but break them entirely. Given that I work in this Industry I’m well informed and know the penalties. I also know that you cannot even “skirt the rules” without lying completely. If this “thing” he is discussing and trying to figure out how to do (or not do rather) if found out, I will absolutely lose my job. 

My spouse is insinuating I’m just up tight and I believe what my Industry tells me but...it’s actually the law. It’s not just my personal beliefs. On the other side though lying like this is completely against my moral code and not something I am okay with. However he keeps pushing that we will talk about it later and he will find a work around so they can’t figure out what was done.   

I got upset and I honestly see him a little differently now and told him if he wants someone “f*** the system” and break the laws with its not going to be me. 

Am I the asshole and uptight?

title: AITA for refusing to let someone join a group start-up business?
text:
Hey everyone, the people in this story are (all of these are fake names) me - me, John - best friend and co-founder, Dean - best friend and co-founder, Lisa - girlfriend and co-founder, and Emma - subject of the story.

So, my 2 best friends, girlfriend, and I are sophomores at a public high school in Texas who are in classes with mostly juniors, with Emma being a junior. John, Dean, Lisa, and I are in a startup company club (idk if anyone knows about this but it's called LaunchX), with ours being being the start-up that won the competition in freshman year. Now, me and John are also heavily involved with stocks, which caused us to be quite good at things like Hackathons and MarketWatch challenges.

Emma joined the LaunchX club this year and she seemed quite interested in getting involved, but then one day, after the first MarketWatch challenge which me and John won, Emma approached us to ask if we could teach her some basics. So, we decided to be nice to a newcomer and help her out. We started by coaching her with some YT videos and started her out on ThinkOrSwim. She literally didn't do anything when we were helping her out and didn't put effort into understanding the software. She expected us analyze and trade for her; we obviously not excited with this idea, made up excuses about why we couldn't teach.

Now after the first round at LaunchX, her 4 man (including her) team's start-up idea plus initial round of testing and analyzing has failed quite spectacularly (no offense, but it was literally a straight rip-off of Google Classroom). But this year (since our group got into the next level of the club from last year), the 3-5 man team who wins the contest, analyzers, and tests get to go on a trip to UT Austin, with a tour and premium camp at their business school. Now that her team has failed, she's trying to join our team. No one in our group wants her to join as we've already seen that she expects others to do her work, she's quite lazy with deadlines, and just wants to ride our success as we came top in the first round. We told her no.

And OH MY GOD, she cried so much it genuinely got scary, she started saying that we ruined her life. She got sent to the counselor and we didn't see her the entire day. Then at the end of the day, her older sister (a senior), walked up to us when we were leaving school as she has to walk home the same way as us. She said that Emma was crying the entire time when she went back home and how she had to console Emma the entire time at the counselor's office. Apparently, UT Austin was her dream and we, being the assholes we are, took that away from her and ruined her life and career.

After this, all four of as we're feeling really bad and guilty, and thinking that we should have given her a second chance. So now I'm feeling both sides of an argument, so I want to know Reddit, AITA? 

Thanks for reading as well, love y'all who spend time trying to help others.

title: AITA for staying friends with a someone who treated a mutual friend very poorly?
text:
TLDR: Best Friend completely relied on Good Friend to take care of something for her while she was incapacitated. Good Friend was unable to do so in a timely manner due to valid personal reasons. Best Friend had no sympathy and cut off the friendship. I maintain my friendship with Best Friend as Good Friend moved away, though in my opinion BF acted in a terrible manner. Am I the Asshole for maintaining this friendship? 

Best Friend had to move houses in a period where she couldn't walk due to a leg surgery. She is completely reliant on a small friend circle. Part of that friend circle was supposed to take care of her during recovery, but they bailed and dropped her just before the surgery was to take place. Friend of friend (FF) helped her out here. The friends who bailed (NF; Not-friends) on Best Friend were supposed to help her move together with FF and Good Friend. Good Friend was only able to help out in the later stages of the move because we were both doing an internship abroad.   
Due to NF bailing and disappearing into the abyss of time, all came to rest of FF. She and her family took care of a lot of things. When Good Friend returned, BF and FF assumed she would take over all matters relating to the move, rather than just 'help'. In my opinion, this assumption was not feasible in any way. GF resides on the other side of the country.   
Short, GF suffered a personal loss and had to take care of a lot of things, including her own move. She still drove to BF and helped her the best she could, while being called lazy, selfish and mocked because she was grieving for 'just a pet'.   
We all know people are different when they're sick, but the abuse hurtled toward GF, BF's entitlement ("That's what friends do for eachother!") and how BF spoke of GF behind her back went beyond all limits.   
Eventually GF called her out. There is only so much abuse you can take, even from someone in unfortunate circumstances. BF lost it, FF lost it, and they both showed an exceedingly ugly side. But even the one-sided  info I got from BF made me side fully with GF. While BF was in need of help and we genuinely all wanted to help her, but BF felt entitled to more than anyone could give.  The friendship was broken off and GF didn't return. BF and FF are not open to discussing GF in any positive way. I stayed friends with BF and I talk to GF as little as I did before, but I feel like a traitor. BF understands me like no other, but in this regard she is blind. While I still love her, I lost a lot of respect for her and feel like the disrespect she showed GF is there for me as well, because among others, I too come from a less high-brow upbringing than BF.   
Am I an asshole for maintaining this friendship?

title: AITA for trying to give a girl advice on her form at the gym?
text:
This has probably been posted a million times but this happened to me today. I go to the gym pretty often and there was this pretty good looking girl there. She was doing deadlifts with her back bend. It was terrible to watch. Her back must have been suffering so bad.

I decided to go up to her and tap her on the shoulder as she had earbuds in her ears. I told her that her form is not optimal and that it’s ruining her back. She told me to go away and stop perving on her. I obviously then left her alone and put my earbuds on and continued my workout. 

AITA?

title: AITA for not talking to my mom and not letting her see my son?
text:
I’m a 25 yr old female and my husband is 25. We have been married for 3 years now and together overall for 8 years. We have a one year old son. Mine and my moms relationship has always been rocking but ever since moving back to my hometown our relationship was really good. After being home for about 3 months and being quarantined for about a month and a half we decided to have 3 friends over to watch a game. I was putting my son to sleep and my mother was blowing up my phone. I texted and saying that I was putting the baby to sleep and she texted back “if you don’t answer the phone right now I’m going to the house” in a very threatening way. So I FaceTimed and I was whispering to her and she started mocking me and asking was I was doing so I told her we had some friends over. She yelled back don’t make me mad and hung up. So I was like WTF lol and she texted me 30 minutes later asking if I wasn’t gonna call her back. I didn’t because SHE hung up on me for no reason. So I didn’t talk to her and the next day we had a very nice family day. We took my son to the park and had dessert. We got back home around 8 and had a small movie night and so I was putting my son to sleep and it was around 10 and she called AGAIN and I texted back “I’m putting the baby to sleep” and she’s like again?!? And I didn’t text back. I was in the room with my son for about an hour and my husband comes in and tells me there’s someone outside and I went into my living room and there was my mother with my in laws. So turns out my mom had talked my husbands mom about how me and my husband were having problems and calling my husband an alcoholic. My husband drinks occasionally a few beers but nothing crazy. She told my mother in law that we were constantly having parties and that my son shouldn’t be growing up in that environment. Everything she said was a lie because first I don’t even drink and my husband drinks occasionally when watching a game or socially, second we had only had a small get together not a party and that was the only time we had done that. My husband basically kicked them all out. The next day we went to talk to her more calmly and she was just saying that she’s never getting in out business and told us she didn’t feel good and to leave. So I told her to call me when she’s ready to talk. It’s been about a month and she still hasn’t contacted me to apologize or to talk about it and only tells my dad to call and tell me to let them see my son. My sons first birthday was 2 weeks ago and I didn’t invite them. So now reddit, AITA??

EDIT: I just wanted to add some more to my post. So my mother has always been this kind of person. This actually hasn't been the first time she has done something like this. She once gathered my husbands parents (we were only dating back then) and went to my apartment in college to "get us in trouble" for going on a birthday trip. I was in college, moved out, over 18, and she didn't pay for anything. Also to add to what she said that day was that she didn't like one of our friends because he drinks. She said that my son can't talk to its up to me to protect him and that he has no right being in an environment like that. Well guess what people!? my dad is an ALCOHOLIC who always drank around us and was constantly drunk. So I know what it's like growing up with someone who drinks and always hated it. I would never let my son go through what I've been through and I've talked to my husband about that.My in laws had no clue she was gonna do what she said, they thought she was genuinely concerned. They have apologized and everything is good with them. Also thank you to everyone telling me I'm not the AH.

title: AITA: Arguing with An Inconsistent Roommate
text:
Without giving away too many details, my roommates and I have recently begun apartment searching for the 2020-2021 academic year.

I have been trying to get the group on the same page since May to start searching, but to no avail. One reason why is that one of our roommates, who we've been asking since April if they wanted to lease a place with us, was always on the fence about it and kept pushing back the decision date. It got to a point where it was mid-June and we had to pretty much demand an answer from them in person so we could move forward.

Flash forward a week, and the search has been hard. We're still confident that we're going to get a place, but now this same roommate is telling us that they don't want to room with us anymore unless we can get a place in a specific area closer to our friends and campus, otherwise they say they could just stay home (they live close by) and drive to school.

They then tried to rush us into applying for a shady apartment we weren't comfortable with for various reasons.

I  had enough and called them the next day to tell them that I'm kinda sick of getting mixed messages from them regarding this situation, and that it was becoming a waste of everyone's time for them to string us along like this and simultaneously rush the rest of us into a bad decision just so we could get this search over with. I felt hurt because it seemed like this person was just abjectly refusing to make a concrete decision and not respecting our time. It was a respectful talk and we're still trying to room with each other, but I've been wondering if I had a right to get into that argument with them.

I know this friend of mine is not a bad person and that they're an adult who can do their own thing, but sometimes I feel like they don't really think things through, and this indecision has cost us a lot of time.

Am I being too sensitive with this? Or is it an appropriate thing to ask of someone? AITA?

title: AITA for telling my husband no more alcohol upstairs after he left it where our toddler could get it?
text: My husband left a cup of coke and some sort of alcohol down from last night. Our toddler got ahold of it and split it all over herself. I told my husband I didn’t want anymore alcohol upstairs because he was acting immature and wasn’t to be trusted to have it upstairs. He tried to say I was only mad because I had to clean up the mess and it’s no different if I left a coke out overnight and she split it. He also tried to tell me I couldn’t make that decision. AITA?

title: AITA for asking people "why" questions?
text:
This is a very small issue I've been reflecting upon for a while and I'd like y'all's opinion on the situation.

I (F25) was raised in a two-daughter household, where my parents (and especially my Dad) never held us back from exploring and researching any topic that interests us. He encouraged us to find the reasoning behind everything and taught us to not accept everything just because "someone said so".

Since COVID cost me the project I was working on, I've been staying with a family friend, Sara, who has graciously housed and fed me for a few months. This family is also hosting another guest, Grandma who's around 70, so that she can stay safe till it's safe enough for her to fly home (an overseas flight, but that isn't important).

Now Grandma, Sara and her husband are all very traditional and religious people (Sara less so, but still more than me by a large margin). Both Grandma and Sara's husband hold a lot of traditional (sometimes outdated) beliefs which I was never taught and therefore don't see any reason to follow. All that is fine, this is a "to each his own" kind of household. 

But every now and then both of them will throw at us some "rules" that are apparently old knowledge and expect us to play along. Sara, through her own bad experiences with her husband, stays mute and plays along whenever she can. I, on the other hand, just ask them "why?" -- Why should we not drink water while eating? Why should dough not be stored a certain way? Why are we not supposed to eat cucumbers in the evenings? My mother used to teach us things like this when we were little, but always with an explanation that she learned, or we ended up finding one ourselves. 

Today when I heard Grandma drop another nugget of knowledge I asked her, "That's interesting, why?" And she just made a sour face and exclaimed, "Ugh not this questioning again!" I simply said, "Well if you're saying it you should at least know why you want us to follow it!" She just huffed and went back to her food, grumbling about my "questioning". 

I genuinely ask these questions out of curiosity, and I find this attitude of certain grown-ups (says I, a 25-year old adult) extremely annoying-- they expect their words to be the law and think asking for explanations is "disrespectful" and "accusatory". Both Grandma and Sara's husband get pissy whenever I ask questions and end up telling me something like "Because that's the way it is."

I feel like I am the AH for "creating mischief" this way, and especially when I ask them to Sara's husband, who is most graciously allowing me to stay in his house. Grandma is just an occasionally grumpy old lady in general but Sara asks me to not "wind her up, she's only here for a little while longer". AITA for asking questions this way?

title: AITA for ignoring my friend's cry for help
text:
So for my 16th birthday we went to Wisconsin Dells (which is a water park for those who are unaware) with a small group of friends. Everything was fine but one day (stayed for the weekend) we were having fun in the hot tub chatting it up breath holding contests and all that but while we were having a nice lighthearted conversation one of my friends suddenly brings up some family problems out of nowhere (either it was a family member passing away or an argument don't remember) Now for more info on the group, we were that kind of misfit group with social problems like for me it was that I had a bit of trouble with empathy and often relied on my friends and family (or in this case Reddit) to tell me if what I did was okay. For my friend it was reading the mood of the room which you can probably gather. Anyways it got quiet for a second and then we just kind of ignored him and carried on because we enjoyed the happiness of the moment and if we stopped and talked about it the mood would've been totally killed. Had he picked the right moment to say it I'm sure all three of us would've acknowledged/talked about it. But just three seconds ago we were debating if Stewart little could beat Remy from Ratatouille in a 1v1 (not really but it was along the lines of that kind of conversation) we didn't want to stop the good time so suddenly so AMITA or in this case AWTA (are we the asshole(friends))?

note: before replying I am aware that the perfect person would always stop to talk about it but please think realistically and imagine yourself in the situation before commenting. Thank you!

&#x200B;

Edit: To everyone saying that I should apologize I couldn't even if I wanted to, that was years ago I don't even know where he is, he could be rich he could be dead I don't know.

title: AITA? My friend says I bullied her even though she’s depressed
text:
One of my best friends, “Kate”, has always struggled with relationships. She’s had a rough childhood. For the past ten years she’s had a “friend with benefits” who she’d like to be in a relationship with. This guy, “John”, has repeatedly told her he could never love her, has often asked her to stop contacting him, told her he has zero interest in relationships etc. Kate thinks he cares about her because he’s sometimes affectionate (eg cooked for her on a couple of occasions)

I and our other close friends tell Kate she’s wasting her time. On occasions she’s acting entirely irrational, eg stalking John’s female friends on social media and throwing fits of jealousy. John has told Kate to listen to her friends and stop wasting her time. However, whenever he’s horny and has no one else to call, he’ll call her because he knows she won’t say no. Even Kate’s counsellor had advised her to stop seeing him.

In the past few years I’ve repeatedly asked Kate to never tell me about him again. She disregards this whenever she has her heart broken. After the last such instance I was really harsh. She then stopped talking about him for a few months and I assumed she finally moved on.

Kate wants children. During covid she’s told us she struggles with being alone and feeling depressed. She has said she’s felt extremely unlucky in life and love. We have tried to be supportive through we are all struggling.

Kate was going to go out with a man she met online. After the date, I texted to ask how it went. She said it didn’t go well and it was her fault. I asked her why and she told me she told her date that she has a friend with benefits (John, who she is still seeing).

I lost it. Told her she’s entirely responsible for being alone and childless because she is sabotaging her own happiness. Told her that maybe she enjoys being lonely and miserable and she likes to play the victim. Also told her that Einstein’s definition of madness is doing the same thing over and over and expecting different outcomes.

She now says I’m a bully, that I made her feel terrible even though I knew she was depressed, and is not talking to me.

AITA?

title: AITA for wanting my roommates to pay for losing my cat?
text:
First want to say sorry for any format or spelling error am on mobile. 

So I(34M) have two roommates (31M , 26M). I have lived with my roommates for little over two years now. I have had (still hoping to find him) my cat for 6 years. My cat has been the most stable thing in my life in that time seeing me through both a very hard divorce and relocating across the country for work where I do not have any family. So you could say he is my emotional support animal. People may think that is weird for a cat but he has always acted more like a dog. 

The day that my cat went missing I had not been feeling well and was sleeping on our couch. While kind of half wake I kept hearing people go in and out of our door. When I did finally wake up enough to go to my bedroom I went to get the cat as I normally do and couldn’t find him. I tore our place apart looking for him as he does respond to his name. I woke up my roommates seeing if he somehow was in one of their rooms. No luck he wasn’t in the house. 

A friend that lives a few house down came over to help me check the neighborhood for him. This being at 4am and I wasn’t even sure how long he had been missing. My friend came back to the house as said he thought he saw him but wasn’t able to catch him.

I later confronted my roommates with the news that one of them had let the cat out and he was missing. One of them (26M) help me to search during the day, put up fliers, knock on doors, and post things online about my cat. My other roommate (31M) has pretty much stopped talking to me since.

It was been over a week now that my cat has been missing. While I am still hopeful to find him. I am having to face the possibility that I won’t and over the past week I have found out just how much his presences or lack there of effects me. My mother suggest that I adopt another cat which I am thinking about. So AITA for thinking that my roommates should have to pay for the adoption fees? 

Edit: to clarify both roommates admitted to leaving the door open while they were outside. From before I even moved in with the cat rules were set that the bathroom door and doors leading outside need to stay shut at all times. This is something everyone agreed to.

TLDR: Roommates let my cat out of the house and is missing. Am thinking about adopting another cat and think they should be the ones to pay for it.

title: AITA For Getting Into Fight With Friend Over Procrastination?
text:
Here's the situation,

I have a small group where me and a few of my close, or at least relatively close friends who I get along with all do writing shit together. Part of the reason I put it together is because I want to make sure I'm making progress while also helping my friends work on their shit too. It's all hobby stuff but we're all still pretty passionate about it.

In comes my friend, love them to pieces, but they has a high HIGH tendency for procrastination. Often times, whenever we would do writing stuff together I would have to or we would discuss about what kind of progress they were making. Which was often really scattered. I would find myself trying to help them stay focused on particular projects, keep them moving (as they admitted several times to me both in DMs and in VC with other present) as they consistently procrastinated.

Now, I'm not a beat around the bush kind of person, I'm blunt in how I talk to my friends and when they want criticism. I told them this that if they wanted my help I was going to be riding their ass so there wouldn't be any surprises. Checking up on their progress, reading what they got, etc, etc. I would be working on my own stuff side by side no it's not just me glaring down at them. Things are good for awhile, they're getting shit done, I'm getting shit done. Everyone's happy.

One day, they tell me they spent all day installing some new system or whatnot on their computer and I mention that I hope they're not procrastinating or avoiding work by doing so as it seemed a waste of time to me anyway. They come back at me saying that I need to stop checking in on them, that they're moving at their own pace and that they do things a certain way in a particular order. Along with some other things mentioning that they did it to sort of climb over an obstacle which I then just destroyed by checking in. Mind you, a few days prior to this very argument they admitted to me they were struggling, couldn't find a healthy pace and were considering some large changes to their projects.

They also went into some large talk about how they have made all this other progress on other things that they didn't mention to me at all and how I need to stop doing what THEY ASKED ME TO DO which was check up on them and make sure they were still progressing.

I admit my response wasn't the calmest, I was pissed that they snapped at me for doing what they asked. It was very much out of the left field. Especially considering beforehand they thanked me for helping them be consistent with output and such. So I snapped back saying that fine if they didn't need my help anymore I wouldn't bother checking in since they had things under control now all of a sudden. They didn't respond to me and haven't responded to my tentative message to reach out (just to make sure they're okay).

 ***"I think i might be the asshole because of snapping at them but I'm not sure."***

AITA?

title: AITA for being mad at my flatmates for messaging Student Support on my behalf
text:
Hey there, this is going to be a bit of a long post as the situation in question is quite hard to explain without leaving out any of the important nuances. 

So I am currently living in a university flat with 5 other flatmates and they have recently teamed up and done something I feel was a little snake-y to me. So I have always struggled with anxiety and have a BPD diagnosis - I see a psychiatrist and am medicated. I am very open about it and I’ve vaguely told my flatmates about it (keep in mind I’m not that close with them). I am also someone who says a lot of self-deprecating humour stuff. I’ve joked about my room being gross all the time - although I would not say that is related to mental illness I am just a very messy person with a very high grosssness tolerance I guess. I also joked abot how once I was drunk and vomited in my drawer and told them before coming to uni I didnt know chicken could not be stored at room temperature and one time I ate chicken with salmonella. Anyway this is all important because they witnessed these behaviours and interpreted it as though I am this crazy person. Moreover I got into a fight with a doctor on the phone and they heard me yelling from my room. That happened a few weeks after one situation in which I had a panic attack over cutting my finger (I am terrified of blood). Lastly this very disgusting maggoty thing appeared in our kitchen bin, I think someone must have put some food in there that got rotten or anything like that. But my flatmates genuinely believed what happened was I freaking SHAT in the bin - “your room is so disgusting you would have done it”. They dont believe me as at this point they think I’m this lunatic bitch.

Well, this morning I woke up to the university having organised an intervention on me and had studentsupport calling me to borderline call me insane. Essentially what ahppened was last night my flatmates went behind my back and emailed them explaining all these situations and ohrasing them as though I am severely mentally ill or whatever. They said I was pooping in bins and screaming like a crazy person and therefore they were intimidated by me and I needed serious support.

Now, I have health anxiety and that was what got me fighting with my doctor and I can be messy and recently i have been having worse anxiety due to tapering the medication I was on. But the thing is I have never fone anything wrong to them, I am always kind and polite and to say they are terrified of me irs just crazy. THey sent this very inflammatory email without even telling me anything beforehand. That way completely robbing of detaining my own narrative. They could have sat me down and told me to talk to student support so i could explain to them in my own terms. Like i DID NOT poop in the bin thats a rraight up lie they told them.

Anyway I got mad and they just talked about how they are worried about me and cant support me the way i need and that right now they are all scared of me and needed to look out for their dafety. I just feel like I was backstabbed when I have always been kind and gentle tothem even more thhan I should have at times. Its just not fair this light I am being painted on. But then they gaslight me w like how i need to admit i have a problem and stuff. Maybe im in the wrong here but idk you tell me.

title: WIBTA if I refused to pay for my in-laws holiday?
text:
So my parents have recently retired and are setting off on a long holiday to the US/Alaska as part of their retirement plan. Over lunch the other week with my in-laws it got brought up and they mentioned how they would've loved to have done a trip like that, but unfortunately they don't have the money. 
Today when my hubby got home from work he said he'd like us to pay (At least 50%) for his parents to do a two week long holiday in the US as well. 
Now we've been pretty strapped financially the past year but we have actually got a bit of money in the bank at the moment, around 8K. We have received $500 off his parents when we were really short for rent one time too. 
I'd be happy to pay around 1-1.5K towards his parents holiday because they're actually super amazing in-laws and I couldn't wish for better. But anymore than that seems too much to me, especially when I feel like we're just getting back on our feet financially. He also wanted to contact some family/friends to see if they would want to pitch in as well. 

I'm the major bread-winner (read: full time work) with hubby working as a casual.

title: AITA for not wanting to attend many of my wife’s Rec League basketball games?
