Am I wrong to want to work but more likely - do other things? Hopefully with her. She doesn’t go to my Rec soccer matches. That is fine. This has been a few years of this. Ideas are welcomed!

title: AITA for using swears as banta in group chats?
text:
Im currently writing this with little to no sleep, but it seems like i might be in the wrong here. I have a friend, who I recently found out has quite controlling and strict with parents. Since I upgraded my phone, my texts havent all saved, and the groups i left too. I like to have banta and send quite dark humour memes to my friends, and 99% of the time they understand its a joke. I found a few copypastas (which had cuss words in them) and thought they were funny, so i sent them. Keep in mind my friend copied and pasted them too, and trys to make edgy jokes aswell. Around 30 minutes ago i got a text from him, saying his mom is ‘livid’ that I used ‘foul language’ in my texts on a particular group chat. It happens that his mom checks his phone every day and saw my messages. He said that he deleted the ones he sent (which he didnt, because they were still there when i checked) and if i didnt delete all of mine his parents would block me and he would get punished. I didnt want to make it worse, so i deleted every single message i sent so there was no messages left. Even the normal ones, saying ‘hi’ or something because i knew that it would be best to be safe. I texted him with an apology, but if I’m honest it wasn’t really heartfelt, and i just wanted to get it over and done with. I know i do go a bit too far sometimes, and i think sometimes i am a bit of a dick, but I dont know if this was me being an asshole, or his parents.

I know this isnt as extreme compared to other posts on here, but with other important things happening it just piles onto my stress and i think if somebody else with a clear mind could tell me if im an asshole it would take a weight off my chest.

Also sorry for typos, its pretty late while I’m writing this.

title: AITA for sticking to my guns on hubby smoking?
text:
We met in 2001, married in 2004. I (44F), and he (51M) both smoked at the time. In talks before marriage we both said it was not something we wanted to do forever, and especially if we ever had kids. 

We now have a 14 year old son. I quit all the way the day I found out I was pregnant and never looked back. He agreed to quit as well.  I don’t remember every little detail over the whole past 14 years, but he’d tell me he quit, then I’d discover he didn’t, he’d tell me again he quit, and it goes on and on like that.

Fast forward to the happenings of the past year.  He has lied to my face two times that he wasn’t smoking when I had the evidence of him smoking in my hands. Goes fucking ballistic and turns it into me being the bad one.  I’ve always believed every word he’s ever told me so the outright lies have really rattled me to the core. The arguments he throws my way are he’s home, not out at bars drinking, so I should be happy about that and leave him to his smoking. I set boundaries to where he cannot smoke around me or at the house. He’s messy so when he did it at home there were butts everywhere outside and ashes all over the back porch.

My main concern is his health and being here long-term for his son and I. He currently takes 3 medications to control his blood pressure (yes, 3!), takes cholesterol meds due to his horrible diet and zero exercise, and is overweight. He travels for work, but when he’s not traveling, he’s home-based. His daily routine has consisted of get up, do our morning routine with the animals and breakfast and our son, then he lays back down on the couch and sleeps for a few hours. He thinks this is okay, nothing wrong with that, but I see it as a sign of his poor health and another reason to get his shit together and quit smoking.

He can go days without smoking, yet says it’s an addiction for him. We went to the mountains, and he didn’t smoke for 3 days straight. He also told me in August, with no prodding from me, that he picked September 1st to quit.  Mid-September I asked how the quitting was going. He totally forgot he’d said that.

I’ve been supportive with ways to deal with quitting from forming new habits, to meds, to whatever he thinks might work. He claims he should be able to do it just fine on his own since he can go days without.

His smoking is now a huge trigger for me. I discovered he purchased a carton of cigs for his current work trip. I lost my shit. We’re currently not speaking. I do all the important shit for our household. He keeps up the mowing of the yard and some interior cleaning. Everything else I do (and work full-time). I feel like the least he could do is what he’s told me over and over he’s going to do. There’s a strong history of him saying he’ll do things that he never does. It’s like same song, different dance.  I’m at the end of my rope.

So, am I the asshole for sticking to my guns and demanding he quit?

title: AITA for not acknowledging the chores my fiance has done around the house?
text:
My fiance (23m) and I (25f) have been living together for a year and a half. The first year that we were living together, we almost broke up because I felt like I was doing all the chores and cooking. We eventually came to an agreement on who would do what chores and everything was okay for a while. After our lease was up, we ended up moving to another place that got extremely messy due to my own personal mental health reasons and because he was also not cleaning. Once I started recovering, I cleaned the entire house by myself. All he did was move some gravy stuff into the garage so that we could have a spare room so his brother could move in with us. Before his brother officially moved in, we made an agreement on who would do what chores and when. He was in charge of cleaning out the cats' litter boxes and pick up the dog poop from the front lawn. We would both clear out the boxes, and we would alternate on who would do laundry, wash dishes, and cook. I would deal with everything else since it really wasn't that much. We soon realized that his brother is messy, doesn't do any chores, and barely knows how to cook.

The question: The day before yesterday I asked him to wash the dishes before work and he washed the dishes in the sink. There were still pots and pans left on the stove but he went to go play video games for 3 hours before getting ready for work. Right when he was getting ready for work, I noticed that he left the pots and pans on the stove. Then yesterday, he did not feed our cats and dog their breakfast. I became very upset at that point. I told him I was thinking of cancelling the wedding because he was not doing the chores he was supposed to do and I was frustrated. I asked him for help with folding clothes since he wasn't folding clothes at all. It's been a month later after I originally asked him and now the clean clothes basket is overflowing with clothes. I told him I refused to fold them since I had been folding them and putting them away by myself for a few months now. Even though we both agreed to do laundry every other time, he has only washed the darks once. Meaning, he hasn't even washed an entire basket of clothes leaving me to do laundry by myself. I explained that the reason we agreed on those chores and what we would do was because he wasn't doing them. I also brought up the issue with him washing dishes but not washing the pots and pans. On top of all that, a week ago he was cleaning the cat litter boxes but did not completely clean one of them and that litter box has been outside since. His argument is that he is doing everything that I'm asking him to do but since they are not up to my standards, I'm not acknowledging the chores that he is actually doing. He stated that he has done everything that I've asked of him but he feels like it is not enough for me. So I want to know, AITA for not acknowledging the chores he has done?

title: AITA for "stealing" my bff's car when he was drunk?
text:
This is very long, sorry. TL;DR at the bottom.
So, this happened a couple months ago, one of my friends was having a bday party and everyone drank until we couldn't even stand straight anymore so no one was smart enough to realize maybe bff shouldn't drive. But when my bf suggested we should go to a bar we all just went with it.

My bff drove way over the speed limit, and I got kinda scared but we arrived safely at the bar. But then the bday girl called us, and told us that we had forgotten to take one of our friends. We were so drunk we forgot a human being.

So, we all got into the car again and went to get her.  On our way to the bday girl's house my bff started to drive way faster, and then he hit a couple in a motorcycle. The girl flew and hit the windshield and it shattered. The guy fell into the ground. After talking to them they seem fine, no serious injuries, just some scratches. My bff freaked out but made a deal so they wouldn't call the cops on us. He gave them 180$.

I told my bff someone else should drive now and the others back me up, but he was angry and still very drunk and told us to shut up and to get in the car. We did it but me and his brother were trying to convince him to stop and let someone else drive.

We got there and my boyfriend and my bff went inside to get our other friend. My bff's brother talked to me and we decided I would drive and we would convince my bff to go in the back. So I got into the driver's seat and told the others to lock the doors (I had a bad feeling about this plan). When the others came back my bff realized what we did and he yelled at us and wouldn't listen when we told him to get into the back seat. He threatened to brake the window, my bf held him and I drove away.

I went to my bff's house and when we got there he and my bf were already there (they got a ride). When I parked he got up and started to hit my window and screamed at me, calling me every name on the book. Before I could go out he pushed to the ground and entered his car and parked it inside and came back. He threatened me and my bf stopped him until his parents got up and took him inside. I went home and when the adrenaline got out of my system I started crying realizing what happened. 

He didn't talked to any of us for nearly a month but then he started talking to the others again, but he never reached out to me. I felt like we owed each other and apology, so after months I reached out to him. I told him I was very sorry and missed him a lot. He told me he accepted my apologies even though they were very late but maybe we could work it out. He never apologize to me so, was I the butthole this whole time?

Edit: I did realize after the fact that I made a mistake driving being intoxicated, but my stupid self thought that was smart at the time.
TL;DR: I stole my best friend's car because he was drunk and crashed into a motorcycle, so I thought he shouldn't drive, but he got mad a me and thinks I'm the only one on fault.

title: AITA for refusing to move my car from a handicapped person’s spot?
text:
I (29F) was staying at my significant other’s house and had my car parked outside, I later noticed a car parked extremely close to me (1 cm) and I complained It would be hard to get out. SO saw my car and told me the car that was parked too close was their disabled housemates car and he always parks there because he has limited mobility due to his disability and said I should move it so he doesn’t get mad. I refused and our friends think I’m the asshole.

I refused to move my car because:
-he was already home for the night and it was 1am and I was blocked in 
-this is not a spot that belongs to him
-this is not a handicapped spot
-this is public street parking in a crowded city

INFO: I didn’t move my car because he was already home, I just told my significant other I wouldn’t park there in the future

title: AITA for not making sure the shower head is facing the proper direction?
text:
So I’m not sure what I do but I guess I leave the shower head facing out of the shower at then end of my showers. My boyfriend is asking me to make sure I leave it the right way so when he goes to turn it on it doesn’t splash outside of the tub. I think it’s his responsibility to make sure it is facing the right direction before turning it on. That’s what I do every time. So, am I the ass hole?

Edit: I don’t leave it pointing out of the shower it is just at an angle where it splashes. And like someone pointed out it’s probably from the water pressure when it’s turned on.

title: AITA for getting upset with my boyfriend because he triggers my eating disorder and doesn't think it's his fault?
text:
Apologies for length, I feel I need to give some background.

I've been bulimic and anorexic for 2 years. I've been with my boyfriend 8 years. I was never overweight. Skinny fat. I'm 5"3 and I've never been over 126 pounds. 


I was always conscious of the fact that boyfriend has always been into incredibly skinny girls with the tiniest waists possible (Miss Bo, Maria Domark) and I feel this added to my disorder over the years. If you've never seen these girls, their photos are often photoshopped beyond anatomical sense...
He's always exclusively liked girls on the slightly underweight side. 


He never lauded these women in my face but when you're with someone that long, I used to see them when he was scrolling through social media. He has always been critical of people who are overweight. Commenting on women's appearance on TV or in real life is normal for him. My boyfriend does NOT like chubby girls or flab. If I was to sit and expose some normal belly rolls he would literally grimace.


My boyfriend didn't know about my eating disorders but always knew I was self conscious about my waist and stomach in particular (wouldn't let him touch it and always covered it up)
When he found out about my problems he was very mad that I never told him. I suggested he look into how to support me through this and avoid triggers. He said he would and he loved me and wanted me to be better. 


I started therapy for the first time in my life just before January, and it opened a huge can of worms for me and then Coronavirus happened and therapy had to stop.


Onto the issue:
Yesterday we were both looking at a photograph on a website on his phone, the picture wouldn't zoom in so I suggested he screenshot and zoom in on the pic in his gallery. He took a screenshot, then dithered and wouldn't open his gallery in front of me. I asked him what was wrong. He said he didn't want to open it up because he had some NSFW photos of girls and he didn't want to upset me. Fair enough. 


I asked him what sort of pictures were they and he mentioned Miss Bo's name. My heart broke. He knows I am going through the worst of my disorder. He knows my waist and stomach is my biggest insecurity. I wouldn't mind but it's never any other body type. It's CLEARLY his kink.
And to have so many pictures of her there that he has to hide it? 


I explained I understand it's a fantasy but imagine if I exclusively looked at sexy pictures of men with MASSIVE feet (he's insecure of his small feet). He said he wouldn't care. I insisted I wouldn't police what he looks at. But Christ does it really have to be exclusively something he KNOWS ruins my life right now? 


Guys, Am I the Asshole here?

title: AITA for befriending a shy new kid in class
text:
When I was in a heavily religious private high school, I was one out of 10 students in a super tiny class so having a new kid come was a fairly big deal. When he arrived, I tried to be nice to him by making sort of cringy dad jokes. The new kid was into basketball, so I decided to call him "basketball kid" instead of his name playfully. I didn't mean any harm by it but low and behold, he left the school shortly after for reasons unknown to me. Later in the year, the teachers started treating me differently and singling me out in terms of trouble. I was trying to understand why I was being treated differently. I was called into a teachers class after school and I was essentially told that the kid said to all the teachers that I horrendousely bullied him and I am the reason why he left. Obviously, he was lying, but part of me feels that I shouldn't have even playfully called him "basketball kid" in the first place. AITA?


Edit: I should also note that he was laughing while I called him this, it was not a bad reaction at all.

title: AITA for not wanting to continue to engage
text: AITA I have lived with my bf for 3 years, his best friend and his wife have been judgmental of my swearing from day one. My bf and I both swear and both tone it way down with them but we do slip from time to time. When our families are together (the children, all 22+ yrs old, are all friends) the children swear a lot around them.  Let me repeat, these are adult children. We have an event coming up and they, the best friend and the wife, called my bf to coach me about my swearing. No one else's just mine. I really don't want to go now, not for me or for spite, but for them. If they don't care for me I don't wish to continue to make them uncomfortable. I am already actively trying to appease them by toning down my language. Not being petty, just thinking why make the event harder on anyone. He thinks I should still go and wants to stay out of it other than that, and I have a problem with that. He should have redirected the best friend to me if he wanted to "stay out of it". He thinks they don't invite us to do more things because of my swearing. I am just amazed. There are times I sit and count how many times someone else says any swear word and no response from anyone. I accidentally say hell and the glares. I am at the point where I just wish it was different but it isn't.

title: AITA For not going to a party?
text:
I am currently a male sophomore at university. During sophomore year of high school I met a girl, Sarah. We became good friends. She also introduced me to a guy a year below us named Adam. Adam quickly became one of my best friends and I enjoyed spending time with both of them.

Throughout sophomore year I began to develop feelings for Sarah. She was the first person I ever had a really big crush on in high school. But Sarah had a boyfriend at the time, so I backed off. 

Eventually however, she was single. In response I tried to get closer to her but I didn’t express my feelings. During these times she would occasionally be weirdly dismissive toward me.

For example, I would take her to a dance and she would agree, but she would completely ignore me the whole time. She would agree to go on a date-like hangout but ask me to not post anything about it. I tried to just give up and stop talking to her, but she would always assure me that she really enjoyed spending time with me.

This continued until Senior year of high school when she told me that she would never date me because she wouldn’t want to risk our friendship. I respected her decision and actually tried to leave her alone, but she didn’t want that.

After graduating high school and going to University, I decided to try to distance myself from Sarah and meet new people. However, Sarah would call me almost every night to inform me of her issues she was having with guys and that stuff.

This confused me as she knew I had feelings for her, but it felt like she was rubbing it in my face that I couldn’t date her by talking about relationship issues. But I tried to be understanding and explained to her that I wasn’t really over her and that talking to me about guys made me feel sad. I understand you should be able to talk to friends about that stuff, but I was ready to stop being friends with her since I couldn’t provide that service.

Sarah took it well and still wanted to be friends with me. I was okay with this until she asked me if I thought she should fuck a guy she was talking to. 

That was my limit, I went to a therapist and explained my situation to them, and they said I should cut off my relationship with Sarah and that she was keeping me around for affirmation, so I did. She was very understanding and told me to take all the time I need.

Now, Adam is graduating high school and is having a graduation party. He invited me... and Sarah. I would never make him choose between his friends so I politely declined the invitation and suggested that we could hang out some other time. But he called me a pussy for being “afraid” of Sarah and that I should just go. And he showed me that he was texting Sarah and she wants me to “get the fuck over it for a couple hours.”

So, I obviously could go and ignore her, but seeing her there would ruin the whole experience for me.

title: AITA for being annoyed at my best friend for breaking our marriage pact?
text:
My best female friend and I have went to elementary, middle, and high school together. We never dated each other since we would rather be best friends for life. We dated other people in high school and that never impacted our friendship. When we were 22 and single, we thought it would be fun to make a pact that if we were both not married by 32, we would get married to each other. We got that idea from a foreign film we saw together. 


We talked about this pact often until she got married when she was 28, had a kid and got divorced two and a half years later. I have had several short-term relationships. I turned 32 in September and her birthday was yesterday. I called her to say happy birthday and mentioned our pact. She said she isn’t ready for this much commitment yet. She talks about her daughter and being busy with work. I told her that I like her daughter and don’t mind taking her in as my own and I’ll give her as much time as she needs, but she told me to please just drop this discussion. 


I got a bit annoyed and asked her what was the purpose of this pact if we are not going to honor it. She says it’s a lot different never having been married than having been married and divorced, that I would never understand her reservations. She says if and when she’s ready to discuss our marriage pact, she will. I told her that’s fine and wished her well. In the meantime, I’m not expecting much out of this marriage pact and will keep dating and finding the one for me.

title: AITA for refusing to disappear from the apartment for the day so my roommate could have the place to himself when his gf arrives?
text:
First, some background. I have two roommates; for the purposes of this post, I will refer to them as R1 and R2.

We live in the UK and R1's girlfriend lives in the states. As far as I know, their relationship has been long distance since day one, they've been together for at least a few years (I think) and they don't see each other often due to the cost of travel. However, they've completed the process to get her a visa with the intention of eventually getting her British citizenship, and she's due to fly over on December 3rd.

As for myself, I work from home full time and don't have much of a social life outside of the internet, so even on weekdays I'm almost always here. Being conscious of that, the thought did cross my mind that R1 might want the place to himself on the 3rd, and when he eventually asked if I'd be around on that date, I said I'd see if I could make plans with R2.

R2 and I had already been planning a 1 or 2 night trip elsewhere in Europe, so I spoke to him to see if coinciding that trip with the 3rd would be viable. Unfortunately it isn't because R2 is working from around midday until midnight on that day and can't take that time off.

Given that the 3rd is a Tuesday and everyone else I know in the area is going to be at work too, I'm not keen on going anywhere at all. I told R1 I would likely be in as a result, to which he asked if I'd disappear if he gave me some money to go get myself some food. Again, I declined because I'm not the sort of person who goes out alone (apart from going to the cinema once or twice per month) or hangs out in a coffee shop to work. I then suggested that since he's willing to spend money on the cause, he could perhaps just book a hotel for the night instead, to which he explained that he wants to cook dinner for his gf. Regardless, he seemed to accept my answer but was visibly (and somewhat understandably) dejected about it.

On top of that, the aforementioned trip I'm taking with R2 is still planned, just on the 9th instead of the 3rd - and I'll be gone for 3-4 weeks as of the 12th to visit family, so R1 will have the place to himself plenty enough pretty soon, even if not as early as he had hoped.

So: AITA for refusing to disappear for the afternoon and evening when my roommate's long distance girlfriend arrives?

**---**

**Edit:** I feel like I should add some more context about the reason I rejected R1's offer of money. Adding this separately from the original post content so people can easily decide whether or not to factor it in to their judgment. Basically, while going to the cinema or working in a coffee shop is a trivial thing to most people, I struggle with doing such things by myself. I have some anxiety problems - nothing severe, but enough to make going out by myself a bit of a challenge. I'm 30 and only convinced myself to start doing such things this year. I have far less of a problem going out or travelling with family and friends, hence I was keen to make plans with R2 or see if anyone else was around to hang out with, as I would have happily taken those options at my own expense. We also live in a village where my only option is to make an express trip into our nearest city - so while there's nothing outright stopping me from going, it's slightly more of an obstacle for me than normal.

Thank you for the comments so far! I appreciate all points of view.

**Edit #2:** This is a partial copy of a reply I made in the comments, but it's pretty much where I stand now. I was expecting mixed responses, but the overall sentiment has surprised me and made me pause to think about whether I should make a bigger effort than I already have.

Thing is, I've lived with previous roommates (with their partners) who fucked in their rooms whenever they felt like it, whether I was in or not. It didn't bother me, I'd just put my headphones on (thank fuck for noise cancelling!), and while I'm not suggesting this roommate should be fine with that too, if he wants a degree of privacy where even me hermitting in my room all evening (which I'm totally prepared to do) isn't enough, I'm not convinced that it's *entirely* on me to go out of my way to give him that degree of privacy.

But the Reddit hivemind has spoken: I'm an asshole. Even after speaking to friends and acquaintances who know me (and the area I live in) better than strangers on the internet, I was seriously prepared to accept that I'm being an asshole and do whatever I can to make up for it. So to that end, I spoke to a few local friends (who, incidentally, believe I am not an asshole) and one of them has very kindly offered to take the afternoon off work so I can go visit him, which is something I've been meaning to do for a while anyway.

Unfortunately, that's not going to help R1 much as it turns out I was mistaken about R2's working hours. He works from early morning until around 6pm, which means he'll be at home during the evening. His stance is very much that we're under no obligation to leave when we wouldn't ordinarily do so, that R1 and his gf are adults who can accept that it's an inconvenience to us and can postpone their special day to the following week when we're actually away. I'm inclined to agree, even though I'll more than likely go ahead with my plans anyway as that at least means one less roommate in the house on terms that also work better for me.

**Edit #3:** I spoke to R1 as well. I actually told him about this post and the responses it got, and he was taken aback. Said I shouldn't worry about it and he's fine with the situation. Nonetheless, I'm sticking to my plan (assuming my friend can clear the time off work).

Thank you to those who gave their legit points of view without being dicks about it. To the others: Fuck you. You're the real assholes here.

title: AITA for wanting the full price for something a friend of mine bought even though it got damaged in shipping and she has financial troubles?
text:
Thiss is a situation that has been going on for over half a year. I sold some Manga to a friend back in late October/early November. I said that it was okay to pay when she could. After the packaged arrived, one of the manga was appearantly damaged. It was water damage, and I usually remember this kind of thing happening to a manga, but the manga was still readable and my friend didn´t ask to lower the price. 

Skip forward to now, she still hasn´t paid and as I´m going to a Conveention soon, I asked her for the money. After that, she asked me if we could lower the price. I explained that I didn´t think of it as fair. The whole package would´ve costed 25 Euros. 7 Euros was packaging, 2,5 Euros was a key chain that she had bought and 8 Euros for two other mangas she bought for a friend. So in full, she paid 7,5 Euros for the three Manga she bought for herself. That is very cheap and I would usually not sell it at this price. Which is why I refused to give her a discount of 3 Euros. 

She then went on to say that her family hardly had any money (which I believe, I don´t have any reason to doubt it) and she had to see when she had money. I replied that it´s okay, I don´t need the money now, but I want it at some point. 

So, this is two AITAs? First, am AITA for demanding the full price even though one of the mangas was damaged in shipping? I don´t need the three Euros, I don´t have any financial troubles, which is why I would say asshole. At the same time, a small part of me says that she already got the mangas very cheap and she then would´ve paid about 4 Euros for Mangas that originially costed about 20. The second AITA is if I´m the asshole for wanting her to send me the money at some point. I obviously can´t force her to send it to me when her family is having financial trouble, even though I definitly would like to end this situation at some point, but yeah, it´s okay if she sends it whenever. AITA?

title: AITA for bringing my Nintendo Switch to a bridal shower?
text: Okay, for context I have super bad anxiety. My mother is forcing me to go to this bridal shower, and I haven't had time to really sit and calm myself since I've been out in public all weekend in large crowds(I live in a tourist based place), and it messes with my anxiety big time. I asked if I could bring my Switch to play so I can keep calm and not have an attack at the bridal shower for my godmother's daughter, and she said it would be disrespectful. Am I the ass?

title: AITA for cleaning the mud off my boots outside?
text:
I live in a house with several other people: my room is on the ground floor.

My job is out of the city, near lots of grass and mud. I often come back with dirt on my boots.

The mud dries and starts to fall off by the morning, so I got into the habit of stepping out of the side door next to my room and stamping around on the concrete area outside to get the dried mud off, so I don't track it inside.

However, one of the other guys has just told me not to do that, because it's right in front of the side door and he thinks people will step on it as they come in, tracking it in anyway. I pointed out there is a mat right inside the foot to wipe your feet, but he didn't care.

He further said that I shouldn't leave anything on the concrete at all, and instead should wipe my boots on the grass or over the drain.

I feel that concrete outside is the perfect place to get the dirt off my boots, and that it would just be extra hassle for me to walk all the way over to the grass to satisfy his sense of tidiness.

So, AITA?

title: WIBTA for reporting a loved teacher close to retirement?
text:
I (15F) am in a Spanish class with no control whatsoever. The teacher,  Ms. H, never gives us any worksheets, spends 40 minutes of class time fooling around with the students, and I haven't learned a thing. I got high 90's last year, now I'm getting 70's. (She gives surprise tests, not quizzes. ) 


My breaking point was last week. She was out for a day, and gave us an essay with no explanation how to do it, although she promised the day before to tell us how to. When she came back, the class asked her about it, and she said it wasn't her fault she was out, as it was the only day she could get tickets to a concert. "You should now how to do it anyway, you could figure it out." Were her words.


I know of 5 people who've tried to drop the class. The problem is that she's the only teacher who does our grade level, and there's no where else to go.


I want to report her, but there's a problem.


She only has 2 years to retirement, and has been working for 30 years, somehow. I don't know if'd get her fired, but it seems to be a nasty move. She's nice enough to most of the class, she scts like everyone's best friend, but she doesn't fo her job.


WIBTA if I said something?
